      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3372:   - Limit the number of pods tracked in a single job sync to avoid starvation of small jobs.\n3373:   - The metric job_pod_finished_total counts the number of finished pods tracked by the job controller (#105197, @alculquicondor) [SIG Apps, Instrumentation and Testing]\n3374: \n3375: ### Failing Test\n3376: \n3377: - Fixes hostpath storage e2e tests within SELinux enabled env (#104551, @Elbehery) [SIG Testing]\n3378: \n3379: ",
            "endLine": 3379,
            "startLine": 3372
          },
          {
            "content": "1586: \n1587: - Kubernetes is now built with Golang 1.17.10 ([#110045](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110045), [@cpanato](https://github.com/cpanato)) [SIG Cloud Provider, Instrumentation, Release and Testing]\n1588: \n1589: ### Failing Test\n1590: \n1591: - Allow KUBE_TEST_REPO_LIST to be a remote url ([#109512](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109512), [@eddiezane](https://github.com/eddiezane)) [SIG Cloud Provider and Testing]\n1592: \n1593: ",
            "endLine": 1593,
            "startLine": 1586
          },
          {
            "content": "2540: \n2541: ### Failing Test\n2542: \n2543: - Fixes hostPath storage E2E tests within SELinux enabled env ([#104551](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/104551), [@Elbehery](https://github.com/Elbehery))\n2544: \n2545: ",
            "endLine": 2545,

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3506: // +enum\n3507: type TaintEffect string\n3508: \n3509: ",
            "endLine": 3509,
            "startLine": 3506
          },
          {
            "content": "3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: ",
            "endLine": 3529,
            "startLine": 3522
          },
          {
            "content": "3513: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3514: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3515: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3516: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3517: ",
            "endLine": 3517,
            "startLine": 3513
          }
        ],

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3174: * fixed an issue that the correct PluginConfig.Args is not passed to the corresponding PluginFactory in kube-scheduler when multiple PluginConfig items are defined. ([#82483](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/82483), [@everpeace](https://github ... [line truncated]\n3175: * Adding TerminationGracePeriodSeconds to the test framework API ([#82170](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/82170), [@vivekbagade](https://github.com/vivekbagade))\n3176: * /test/e2e/framework: Adds a flag \"non-blocking-taints\" which allows tests to run in environments with tainted nodes. String value should be a comma-separated list. ([#81043](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/81043), [@johnSchnake](https://git ... [line truncated]",
            "endLine": 3176,
            "startLine": 3174
          },
          {
            "content": "1804: #### Network\n1805: - EndpointSlices: If upgrading a cluster with EndpointSlices already enabled, any EndpointSlices that should be managed by the EndpointSlice controller should have a `endpointslice.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label set to `endpointslice-controller.k8s.io`.\n1806: \n1807: ",
            "endLine": 1807,
            "startLine": 1804
          },
          {
            "content": "3136: * Resolves bottleneck in internal API server communication that can cause increased goroutines and degrade API Server performance ([#80465](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80465), [@answer1991](https://github.com/answer1991))\n3137: * The deprecated mondo `kubernetes-test` tarball is no longer built. Users running Kubernetes e2e tests should use the `kubernetes-test-portable` and `kubernetes-test-{OS}-{ARCH}` tarballs instead. ([#83093](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83 ... [line truncated]\n3138: * Improved performance of kube-proxy with EndpointSlice enabled with more efficient sorting.  ([#83035](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83035), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\n3139: * New APIs to allow adding/removing pods from pre-calculated prefilter state in the scheduling framework ([#82912](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/82912), [@ahg-g](https://github.com/ahg-g))\n3140: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 3142,
            "startLine": 3136
          }

          {
            "content": "3893:   - 'watch': indicates that it is a watch request ([#105517](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/105517), [@tkashem](https://github.com/tkashem)) [SIG API Machinery and Instrumentation]\n3894: - Add a test to guarantee that conformance clusters require at least 2 untainted nodes ([#106313](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/106313), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Architecture and Testing]\n3895: - Allow attached volumes to be mounted quicker by skipping exp. backoff when checking for reported-in-use volumes ([#106853](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/106853), [@gnufied](https://github.com/gnufied)) [SIG Apps, Node and Storage]\n3896: - An inefficient lock in EndpointSlice controller metrics cache has been reworked. Network programming latency may be significantly reduced in certain scenarios, especially in clusters with a large number of Services. ([#107091](https://github.com/kubernet ... [line truncated]\n3897: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 3901,
            "startLine": 3893
          },
          {
            "content": "2664: - Added a new label `type` to `apiserver_flowcontrol_request_execution_seconds` metric - it has the following values: - 'regular': indicates that it is a non long running request - 'watch': indicates that it is a watch request. ([#105517](https://github.co ... [line truncated]\n2665: - Added a test to guarantee that conformance clusters require at least 2 untainted nodes. ([#106313](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/106313), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea))\n2666: - Adds PV deletion protection finalizer only when PV reclaimPolicy is Delete for dynamically provisioned volumes. ([#109205](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109205), [@deepakkinni](https://github.com/deepakkinni))\n2667: ",
            "endLine": 2667,
            "startLine": 2664
          },
          {
            "content": "2105: \n2106: - Fix a bug on endpointslices tests comparing the wrong metrics ([#110920](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110920), [@jluhrsen](https://github.com/jluhrsen)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n2107: - Fix a bug that caused the wrong result length when using --chunk-size and --selector together ([#110735](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110735), [@Abirdcfly](https://github.com/Abirdcfly)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]\n2108: - Fix bug that prevented the job controller from enforcing activeDeadlineSeconds when set ([#110544](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110544), [@harshanarayana](https://github.com/harshanarayana)) [SIG Apps]\n2109: ",
            "endLine": 2109,
            "startLine": 2105

            "content": "2503: \n2504: var map_Taint = map[string]string{\n2505: \t\"\":          \"The node this Taint is attached to has the \\\"effect\\\" on any pod that does not tolerate the Taint.\",\n2506: \t\"key\":       \"Required. The taint key to be applied to a node.\",\n2507: \t\"value\":     \"The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\",\n2508: \t\"effect\":    \"Required. The effect of the taint on pods that do not tolerate the taint. Valid effects are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\",\n2509: \t\"timeAdded\": \"TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added. It is only written for NoExecute taints.\",\n2510: }\n2511: ",
            "endLine": 2511,
            "startLine": 2503
          },
          {
            "content": "2516: var map_Toleration = map[string]string{\n2517: \t\"\":                  \"The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\",\n2518: \t\"key\":               \"Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\",\n2519: \t\"operator\":          \"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a particular category.\",\n2520: \t\"value\":             \"Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\",\n2521: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2524,
            "startLine": 2516
          },
          {
            "content": "1271: \t\"unschedulable\": \"Unschedulable controls node schedulability of new pods. By default, node is schedulable. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/nodes/node/#manual-node-administration\",\n1272: \t\"taints\":        \"If specified, the node's taints.\",\n1273: \t\"configSource\":  \"Deprecated: Previously used to specify the source of the node's configuration for the DynamicKubeletConfig feature. This feature is removed.\",\n1274: ",
            "endLine": 1274,
            "startLine": 1271
          }
        ],

}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package helper\\n18: \\n19: import v1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n20: \\n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n24: \\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n25: \\t\\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\\n26: \\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\\n27: \\t}\\n28: }\\n29: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package helper\n18: \n19: import v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n20: \n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n24: \treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n25: \t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\n26: \t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\n27: \t}\n28: }\n29: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go"
  }
}

Taint.\\\",\\n2506: \\t\\\"key\\\":       \\\"Required. The taint key to be applied to a node.\\\",\\n2507: \\t\\\"value\\\":     \\\"The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\\\",\\n2508: \\t\\\"effect\\\":    \\\"Required. It is only written for NoExecute taints.\\\",\\n2510: }\\n2511: \",\"endLine\":2511,\"startLine\":2503},{\"content\":\"2516: var map_Toleration = map[string]string{\\n2517: \\t\\\"\\\":                  \\\"The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\\\",\\n2518: \\t\\\"key\\\":               \\\"Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\\\",\\n2519: \\t\\\"operator\\\":          \\\"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a particular category.\\\",\\n2520: \\t\\\"value\\\":             \\\"Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. (This already\\n3616: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":3620,\"startLine\":3609},{\"content\":\"598: \\n599: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106136, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\\n600: - Fix concurrent map access causing panics when logging timed-out API calls. (#106124, @marseel) [SIG API Machinery]\\n601: \",\"endLine\":601,\"startLine\":598},{\"content\":\"1817: \\n1818: ### CronJob controller v2 is available through feature gate\\n1819: \\n1820: An alternative implementation of the `CronJob` controller is now available as an alpha feature in this release, which has experimental performance improvement by using informers instead of polling. While this will be the default behavior in the future, you ... [line truncated]\\n1821: \\n1822: \",\"endLine\":1822,\"startLine\":1817}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3005: - The Pod `spec.podOS` field is promoted to GA. The `IdentifyPodOS` feature gate unconditionally enabled, and will no longer be accepted as a `--feature-gates` parameter in 1.27. ([#111229](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/111229), [@ravisanto ... [line truncated]\\n3006: - The command line flag `enable-taint-manager` for kube-controller-manager is deprecated and will be removed in 1.26.\\n3007:   The feature that it supports, taint based eviction, is enabled by default and will continue to be implicitly enabled when the flag is removed. ([#111411](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/111411), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculqui ... [line truncated]\\n3008: - [Ephemeral Containers](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/ephemeral-containers/) are now generally available. The `EphemeralContainers` feature gate is always enabled and should be removed from `--feature-gates` flag on the kube-apiserver ... [line truncated]\\n3009: \",\"endLine\":3009,\"startLine\":3005},{\"content\":\"3364: \\n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n3369: \\n3370: \",\"endLine\":3370,\"startLine\":3364},{\"content\":\"2993:   - PreemptionByKubeScheduler (Pod preempted by kube-scheduler)\\n2994:   - DeletionByTaintManager (Pod deleted by taint manager due to NoExecute taint)\\n2995:   - EvictionByEvictionAPI (Pod evicted by Eviction API)\\n2996: \",\"endLine\":2996,\"startLine\":2993}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"471: - name: pod_deletions_total\\n472:   subsystem: taint_eviction_controller\\n473:   help: Total number of Pods deleted by TaintEvictionController since its start.\\n474:   type: Counter\\n475: \",\"endLine\":475,\"startLine\":471},{\"content\":\"452: - name: pod_deletion_duration_seconds\\n453:   subsystem: taint_eviction_controller\\n454:   help: Latency, in seconds, between the time when a taint effect has been activated\\n455:     for the Pod and its deletion via TaintEvictionController.\\n456:   type: Histogram\\n457: \",\"endLine\":457,\"startLine\":452},{\"content\":\"410:   help: Number of errors encountered when forcefully deleting the pods since the Pod\\n411:     GC Controller started.\\n412:   type: Counter\\n413: \",\"endLine\":413,\"startLine\":410}],\"file\":\"test/instrumentation/documentation/documentation-list.yaml\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/test/instrumentation/documentation/documentation-list.yaml\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2330: - Added `apiserver_longrunning_requests` metric to replace the soon to be deprecated `apiserver_longrunning_gauge` metric. ([#103799](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/103799), [@jyz0309](https://github.com/jyz0309))\\n2331: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\\n2332:   - `--port`\\n2333: \",\"endLine\":2333,\"startLine\":2330},{\"content\":\"3630: \\n3631: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\\n3632:   - `--port`\\n3633: \",\"endLine\":3633,\"startLine\":3630},{\"content\":\"3154:   If users experiment problems with stale connections  using HTTP1.1, they can force the previous behavior of the kubelet by setting the environment variable DISABLE_HTTP2. (#104844, @aojea) [SIG API Machinery, Auth and Node]\\n3155: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#105997, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\\n3156: - Enhanced event messages for pod failed for exec probe timeout (#106201, @yxxhero) [SIG Node]\\n3157: \",\"endLine\":3157,\"startLine\":3154}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1034: \\n1035: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106135, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\\n1036: - Fix a bug that `--disabled-metrics` doesn't function well. (#106391, @Huang-Wei) [SIG API Machinery, Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]\\n1037: \",\"endLine\":1037,\"startLine\":1034},{\"content\":\"2273: - Count pod overhead against an entity's ResourceQuota ([#99600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99600), [@gjkim42](https://github.com/gjkim42)) [SIG API Machinery and Node]\\n2274: - EndpointSlice controllers are less likely to create duplicate EndpointSlices. ([#100103](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/100103), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n2275: - Ensure only one LoadBalancer rule is created when HA mode is enabled ([#99825](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99825), [@feiskyer](https://github.com/feiskyer)) [SIG Cloud Provider]\\n2276: - Fixed a race condition on API server startup ensuring previously created webhook configurations are effective before the first write request is admitted. ([#95783](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95783), [@roycaihw](https://github.com/royca ... [line truncated]\\n2277: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2278,\"startLine\":2273},{\"content\":\"1952: - Creating a PVC with DataSource should fail for non-CSI plugins. ([#97086](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/97086), [@xing-yang](https://github.com/xing-yang)) [SIG Apps and Storage]\\n1953: - EndpointSlice controller is now less likely to emit FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices events. ([#99345](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99345), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n1954: - EndpointSlice controllers are less likely to create duplicate EndpointSlices. ([#100103](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/100103), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n1955: - EndpointSliceMirroring controller is now less likely to emit FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices events. ([#99756](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99756), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n1956: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":1958,\"startLine\":1952}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1804: #### Network\\n1805: - EndpointSlices: If upgrading a cluster with EndpointSlices already enabled, any EndpointSlices that should be managed by the EndpointSlice controller should have a `endpointslice.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label set to `endpointslice-controller.k8s.io`.\\n1806: \\n1807: \",\"endLine\":1807,\"startLine\":1804},{\"content\":\"783: - Kube-apiserver: fixed a bug returning inconsistent results from list requests which set a field or label selector and set a paging limit ([#94002](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94002), [@wojtek-t](https://github.com/wojtek-t)) [SIG API Ma ... [line truncated]\\n784: - The EndpointSlice controller now waits for EndpointSlice and Node caches to be synced before starting. ([#94086](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94086), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n785: - The audit event sourceIPs list will now always end with the IP that sent the request directly to the API server. ([#87167](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/87167), [@tallclair](https://github.com/tallclair)) [SIG API Machinery and Auth]\\n786: \",\"endLine\":786,\"startLine\":783},{\"content\":\"2120: - Significant kube-proxy performance improvements for non UDP ports. ([#83208](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83208), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2121: - Improved performance of kube-proxy with EndpointSlice enabled with more efficient sorting. ([#83035](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83035), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2122: - EndpointSlices are now beta for better Network Endpoint performance at scale. ([#84390](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84390), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2123: - Updated EndpointSlices to use PublishNotReadyAddresses from Services. ([#84573](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84573), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2124: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2131,\"startLine\":2120}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2785: - E2e tests wait for `kube-root-ca.crt` to be populated in namespaces for use with projected service account tokens, reducing delays starting those test pods and errors in the logs. ([#107763](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107763), [@smarte ... [line truncated]\\n2786: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\\n2787: - Fixed default config flags for `NewDefaultKubectlCommand`. ([#107131](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107131), [@jonnylangefeld](https://github.com/jonnylangefeld))\\n2788: \",\"endLine\":2788,\"startLine\":2785},{\"content\":\"3536: - API server's deprecated `--target-ram-mb` flag is now removed. ([#108457](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/108457), [@ialidzhikov](https://github.com/ialidzhikov)) [SIG API Machinery, Cloud Provider, Scalability and Testing]\\n3537: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\\n3538: - Improve error message when applying CRDs before the CRD exists in a cluster ([#107363](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107363), [@eddiezane](https://github.com/eddiezane)) [SIG CLI]\\n3539: \",\"endLine\":3539,\"startLine\":3536},{\"content\":\"2301:   JobTrackingWithFinalizers is still disabled by default. ([#109486](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109486), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor)) [SIG Apps and Testing]\\n2302: - Kubeadm: only taint control plane nodes when the legacy \\\"master\\\" taint is present. This avoids a bug where \\\"kubeadm upgrade\\\" will re-taint a control plane node with the new \\\"control plane\\\" taint even if the user explicitly untainted the node. ([#109841]( ... [line truncated]\\n2303: \\n2304: \",\"endLine\":2304,\"startLine\":2301}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3769: \\n3770: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\\n3771:   - `--port`\\n3772: \",\"endLine\":3772,\"startLine\":3769},{\"content\":\"2236: \\n2237: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\\n2238:   - `--port`\\n2239: \",\"endLine\":2239,\"startLine\":2236},{\"content\":\"1771: \\n1772: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106132, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\\n1773: - Fix a bug that `--disabled-metrics` doesn't function well. (#105793, @Huang-Wei) [SIG API Machinery, Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]\\n1774: \",\"endLine\":1774,\"startLine\":1771}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1421: - Migrated `pod-security-admission` to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#114471](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/114471), [@Namanl2001](https://github.com/Namanl2 ... [line truncated]\\n1422: - Migrated controller functions to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116930](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116930), [@fatsheep9146](https://github.com/fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\\n1423: - Migrated the Job controller (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116910](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116910), [@fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\\n1424: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":1427,\"startLine\":1421},{\"content\":\"2264: - Kubernetes is now built with Go 1.20.6 ([#119324](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/119324), [@xmudrii](https://github.com/xmudrii)) [SIG API Machinery, Auth, Cloud Provider, Release and Testing]\\n2265: - Migrate `pkg/controller/endpoint` to contextual logging ([#116755](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116755), [@my-git9](https://github.com/my-git9)) [SIG Apps, Instrumentation and Network]\\n2266: - Migrated the `EndpointSlice` and `EndpointSliceMirroring` controllers (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#115295](https://github.com/kuber ... [line truncated]\\n2267: - Move non-graceful node shutdown to GA. ([#118228](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118228), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory)) [SIG Apps, Storage and Testing]\\n2268: \",\"endLine\":2268,\"startLine\":2264},{\"content\":\"1682: - `kube-apiserver`: Improved memory use when performing `GetList` on the cache. ([#116327](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116327), [@sxllwx](https://github.com/sxllwx))\\n1683: - `kube-controller-manager` and `cloud-controller-manager` have changed the\\n1684:   name of controllers that can be turned on/off that are passed to the `--controllers`\\n1685:   flag (e.g., `pod-garbage-collector-controller`). The old names (eg `podgc`) are\\n1686:   also accepted and aliased to the new names. ([#115813](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/115813), [@atiratree](https://github.com/atiratree))\\n1687: \",\"endLine\":1687,\"startLine\":1682}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3162: \n3163: // TaintEffect defines the effects of Taint\n3164: type TaintEffect string\n3165: \n3166: // These are valid values for TaintEffect\n3167: const (\n3168: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3169: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3170: ",
            "endLine": 3170,
            "startLine": 3162
          },
          {
            "content": "3180: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3181: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3182: \n3183: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3184: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3185: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3186: )\n3187: ",
            "endLine": 3187,
            "startLine": 3180
          },
          {
            "content": "3171: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3172: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3173: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3174: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3175: ",
            "endLine": 3175,
            "startLine": 3171
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/apis/core/types.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/types.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3506: // +enum\n3507: type TaintEffect string\n3508: \n3509: ",
            "endLine": 3509,
            "startLine": 3506
          },
          {
            "content": "3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: ",
            "endLine": 3529,
            "startLine": 3522
          },
          {
            "content": "3513: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3514: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3515: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3516: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3517: ",
            "endLine": 3517,
            "startLine": 3513
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3857: \n3858: func validateTaintEffect(effect *core.TaintEffect, allowEmpty bool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n3859: \tif !allowEmpty && len(*effect) == 0 {\n3860: \t\treturn field.ErrorList{field.Required(fldPath, \"\")}\n3861: ",
            "endLine": 3861,
            "startLine": 3857
          },
          {
            "content": "3863: \tallErrors := field.ErrorList{}\n3864: \tswitch *effect {\n3865: \t// TODO: Replace next line with subsequent commented-out line when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n3866: \tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n3867: \t\t// case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n3868: \tdefault:\n3869: \t\tvalidValues := []core.TaintEffect{\n3870: \t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n3871: \t\t\tcore.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n3872: \t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n3873: \t\t\t// TODO: Uncomment this block when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n3874: \t\t\t// core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit,\n3875: \t\t}\n3876: \t\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *effect, validValues))\n3877: \t}\n3878: ",
            "endLine": 3878,
            "startLine": 3863
          },
          {
            "content": "6025: \n6026: \tuniqueTaints := map[core.TaintEffect]sets.Set[string]{}\n6027: \n6028: \tfor i, currTaint := range taints {\n6029: \t\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\n6030: \t\t// validate the taint key\n6031: \t\tallErrors = append(allErrors, unversionedvalidation.ValidateLabelName(currTaint.Key, idxPath.Child(\"key\"))...)\n6032: \t\t// validate the taint value\n6033: \t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(currTaint.Value); len(errs) != 0 {\n6034: \t\t\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\"value\"), currTaint.Value, strings.Join(errs, \";\")))\n6035: \t\t}\n6036: \t\t// validate the taint effect\n6037: \t\tallErrors = append(allErrors, validateTaintEffect(&currTaint.Effect, false, idxPath.Child(\"effect\"))...)\n6038: \n6039: \t\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n6040: \t\tif len(uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect].Has(currTaint.Key) {\n6041: \t\t\tduplicatedError := field.Duplicate(idxPath, currTaint)\n6042: \t\t\tduplicatedError.Detail = \"taints must be unique by key and effect pair\"\n6043: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 6044,
            "startLine": 6025
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "725: \t//\n726: \t// Decouples Taint Eviction Controller, performing taint-based Pod eviction, from Node Lifecycle Controller.\n727: \tSeparateTaintEvictionController featuregate.Feature = \"SeparateTaintEvictionController\"\n728: \n729: ",
            "endLine": 729,
            "startLine": 725
          },
          {
            "content": "1188: \n1189: \tSeparateTaintEvictionController: {Default: true, PreRelease: featuregate.Beta},\n1190: \n1191: ",
            "endLine": 1191,
            "startLine": 1188
          },
          {
            "content": "174: \t// alpha: v1.27\n175: \t// Enable webhooks in cloud controller manager\n176: \tCloudControllerManagerWebhook featuregate.Feature = \"CloudControllerManagerWebhook\"\n177: \n178: ",
            "endLine": 178,
            "startLine": 174
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/features/kube_features.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/features/kube_features.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "5166: \n5167: \t// to print taints in the sorted order\n5168: \tsort.Slice(taints, func(i, j int) bool {\n5169: \t\tcmpKey := func(taint corev1.Taint) string {\n5170: \t\t\treturn string(taint.Effect) + \",\" + taint.Key\n5171: \t\t}\n5172: \t\treturn cmpKey(taints[i]) < cmpKey(taints[j])\n5173: \t})\n5174: ",
            "endLine": 5174,
            "startLine": 5166
          },
          {
            "content": "3145: \n3146: // EndpointSliceDescriber generates information about an EndpointSlice.\n3147: type EndpointSliceDescriber struct {\n3148: \tclientset.Interface\n3149: ",
            "endLine": 3149,
            "startLine": 3145
          },
          {
            "content": "2141: \n2142: // ReplicationControllerDescriber generates information about a replication controller\n2143: // and the pods it has created.\n2144: type ReplicationControllerDescriber struct {\n2145: \tclientset.Interface\n2146: ",
            "endLine": 2146,
            "startLine": 2141
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/describe/describe.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/describe/describe.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2503: \n2504: var map_Taint = map[string]string{\n2505: \t\"\":          \"The node this Taint is attached to has the \\\"effect\\\" on any pod that does not tolerate the Taint.\",\n2506: \t\"key\":       \"Required. The taint key to be applied to a node.\",\n2507: \t\"value\":     \"The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\",\n2508: \t\"effect\":    \"Required. The effect of the taint on pods that do not tolerate the taint. Valid effects are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\",\n2509: \t\"timeAdded\": \"TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added. It is only written for NoExecute taints.\",\n2510: }\n2511: ",
            "endLine": 2511,
            "startLine": 2503
          },
          {
            "content": "2516: var map_Toleration = map[string]string{\n2517: \t\"\":                  \"The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\",\n2518: \t\"key\":               \"Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\",\n2519: \t\"operator\":          \"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a particular category.\",\n2520: \t\"value\":             \"Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\",\n2521: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2524,
            "startLine": 2516
          },
          {
            "content": "2010: \n2011: var map_ReplicationController = map[string]string{\n2012: \t\"\":         \"ReplicationController represents the configuration of a replication controller.\",\n2013: \t\"metadata\": \"If the Labels of a ReplicationController are empty, they are defaulted to be the same as the Pod(s) that the replication controller manages. Standard object's metadata. More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architectu ... [line truncated]\n2014: \t\"spec\":     \"Spec defines the specification of the desired behavior of the replication controller. More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#spec-and-status\",\n2015: \t\"status\":   \"Status is the most recently observed status of the replication controller. This data may be out of date by some window of time. Populated by the system. Read-only. More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/ap ... [line truncated]\n2016: }\n2017: ",
            "endLine": 2017,
            "startLine": 2010
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types_swagger_doc_generated.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types_swagger_doc_generated.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3609: - Fixed kubelet creating extra sandbox for pods with RestartPolicyOnFailure after all containers succeeded ([#92614](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92614), [@tnqn](https://github.com/tnqn)) [SIG Node and Testing]\n3610: - Fixed memory leak in endpointSliceTracker ([#92838](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92838), [@tnqn](https://github.com/tnqn)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n3611: - Fixed node data lost in kube-scheduler for clusters with imbalance on number of nodes across zones ([#93355](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/93355), [@maelk](https://github.com/maelk)) [SIG Scheduling]\n3612: - Fixed the EndpointSliceController to correctly create endpoints for IPv6-only pods.\n3613:   \n3614:   Fixed the EndpointController to allow IPv6 headless services, if the IPv6DualStack\n3615:   feature gate is enabled, by specifying `ipFamily: IPv6` on the service. (This already\n3616: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 3620,
            "startLine": 3609
          },
          {
            "content": "598: \n599: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106136, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\n600: - Fix concurrent map access causing panics when logging timed-out API calls. (#106124, @marseel) [SIG API Machinery]\n601: ",
            "endLine": 601,
            "startLine": 598
          },
          {
            "content": "1817: \n1818: ### CronJob controller v2 is available through feature gate\n1819: \n1820: An alternative implementation of the `CronJob` controller is now available as an alpha feature in this release, which has experimental performance improvement by using informers instead of polling. While this will be the default behavior in the future, you ... [line truncated]\n1821: \n1822: ",
            "endLine": 1822,
            "startLine": 1817
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3005: - The Pod `spec.podOS` field is promoted to GA. The `IdentifyPodOS` feature gate unconditionally enabled, and will no longer be accepted as a `--feature-gates` parameter in 1.27. ([#111229](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/111229), [@ravisanto ... [line truncated]\n3006: - The command line flag `enable-taint-manager` for kube-controller-manager is deprecated and will be removed in 1.26.\n3007:   The feature that it supports, taint based eviction, is enabled by default and will continue to be implicitly enabled when the flag is removed. ([#111411](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/111411), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculqui ... [line truncated]\n3008: - [Ephemeral Containers](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/ephemeral-containers/) are now generally available. The `EphemeralContainers` feature gate is always enabled and should be removed from `--feature-gates` flag on the kube-apiserver ... [line truncated]\n3009: ",
            "endLine": 3009,
            "startLine": 3005
          },
          {
            "content": "3364: \n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n3369: \n3370: ",
            "endLine": 3370,
            "startLine": 3364
          },
          {
            "content": "2993:   - PreemptionByKubeScheduler (Pod preempted by kube-scheduler)\n2994:   - DeletionByTaintManager (Pod deleted by taint manager due to NoExecute taint)\n2995:   - EvictionByEvictionAPI (Pod evicted by Eviction API)\n2996: ",
            "endLine": 2996,
            "startLine": 2993
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "471: - name: pod_deletions_total\n472:   subsystem: taint_eviction_controller\n473:   help: Total number of Pods deleted by TaintEvictionController since its start.\n474:   type: Counter\n475: ",
            "endLine": 475,
            "startLine": 471
          },
          {
            "content": "452: - name: pod_deletion_duration_seconds\n453:   subsystem: taint_eviction_controller\n454:   help: Latency, in seconds, between the time when a taint effect has been activated\n455:     for the Pod and its deletion via TaintEvictionController.\n456:   type: Histogram\n457: ",
            "endLine": 457,
            "startLine": 452
          },
          {
            "content": "410:   help: Number of errors encountered when forcefully deleting the pods since the Pod\n411:     GC Controller started.\n412:   type: Counter\n413: ",
            "endLine": 413,
            "startLine": 410
          }
        ],
        "file": "test/instrumentation/documentation/documentation-list.yaml",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/test/instrumentation/documentation/documentation-list.yaml"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2330: - Added `apiserver_longrunning_requests` metric to replace the soon to be deprecated `apiserver_longrunning_gauge` metric. ([#103799](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/103799), [@jyz0309](https://github.com/jyz0309))\n2331: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\n2332:   - `--port`\n2333: ",
            "endLine": 2333,
            "startLine": 2330
          },
          {
            "content": "3630: \n3631: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\n3632:   - `--port`\n3633: ",
            "endLine": 3633,
            "startLine": 3630
          },
          {
            "content": "3154:   If users experiment problems with stale connections  using HTTP1.1, they can force the previous behavior of the kubelet by setting the environment variable DISABLE_HTTP2. (#104844, @aojea) [SIG API Machinery, Auth and Node]\n3155: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#105997, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\n3156: - Enhanced event messages for pod failed for exec probe timeout (#106201, @yxxhero) [SIG Node]\n3157: ",
            "endLine": 3157,
            "startLine": 3154
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1034: \n1035: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106135, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\n1036: - Fix a bug that `--disabled-metrics` doesn't function well. (#106391, @Huang-Wei) [SIG API Machinery, Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]\n1037: ",
            "endLine": 1037,
            "startLine": 1034
          },
          {
            "content": "2273: - Count pod overhead against an entity's ResourceQuota ([#99600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99600), [@gjkim42](https://github.com/gjkim42)) [SIG API Machinery and Node]\n2274: - EndpointSlice controllers are less likely to create duplicate EndpointSlices. ([#100103](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/100103), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n2275: - Ensure only one LoadBalancer rule is created when HA mode is enabled ([#99825](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99825), [@feiskyer](https://github.com/feiskyer)) [SIG Cloud Provider]\n2276: - Fixed a race condition on API server startup ensuring previously created webhook configurations are effective before the first write request is admitted. ([#95783](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95783), [@roycaihw](https://github.com/royca ... [line truncated]\n2277: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2278,
            "startLine": 2273
          },
          {
            "content": "1952: - Creating a PVC with DataSource should fail for non-CSI plugins. ([#97086](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/97086), [@xing-yang](https://github.com/xing-yang)) [SIG Apps and Storage]\n1953: - EndpointSlice controller is now less likely to emit FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices events. ([#99345](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99345), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n1954: - EndpointSlice controllers are less likely to create duplicate EndpointSlices. ([#100103](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/100103), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n1955: - EndpointSliceMirroring controller is now less likely to emit FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices events. ([#99756](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99756), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n1956: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 1958,
            "startLine": 1952
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1804: #### Network\n1805: - EndpointSlices: If upgrading a cluster with EndpointSlices already enabled, any EndpointSlices that should be managed by the EndpointSlice controller should have a `endpointslice.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label set to `endpointslice-controller.k8s.io`.\n1806: \n1807: ",
            "endLine": 1807,
            "startLine": 1804
          },
          {
            "content": "783: - Kube-apiserver: fixed a bug returning inconsistent results from list requests which set a field or label selector and set a paging limit ([#94002](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94002), [@wojtek-t](https://github.com/wojtek-t)) [SIG API Ma ... [line truncated]\n784: - The EndpointSlice controller now waits for EndpointSlice and Node caches to be synced before starting. ([#94086](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94086), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n785: - The audit event sourceIPs list will now always end with the IP that sent the request directly to the API server. ([#87167](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/87167), [@tallclair](https://github.com/tallclair)) [SIG API Machinery and Auth]\n786: ",
            "endLine": 786,
            "startLine": 783
          },
          {
            "content": "2120: - Significant kube-proxy performance improvements for non UDP ports. ([#83208](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83208), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\n2121: - Improved performance of kube-proxy with EndpointSlice enabled with more efficient sorting. ([#83035](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83035), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\n2122: - EndpointSlices are now beta for better Network Endpoint performance at scale. ([#84390](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84390), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\n2123: - Updated EndpointSlices to use PublishNotReadyAddresses from Services. ([#84573](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84573), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\n2124: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2131,
            "startLine": 2120
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2785: - E2e tests wait for `kube-root-ca.crt` to be populated in namespaces for use with projected service account tokens, reducing delays starting those test pods and errors in the logs. ([#107763](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107763), [@smarte ... [line truncated]\n2786: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\n2787: - Fixed default config flags for `NewDefaultKubectlCommand`. ([#107131](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107131), [@jonnylangefeld](https://github.com/jonnylangefeld))\n2788: ",
            "endLine": 2788,
            "startLine": 2785
          },
          {
            "content": "3536: - API server's deprecated `--target-ram-mb` flag is now removed. ([#108457](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/108457), [@ialidzhikov](https://github.com/ialidzhikov)) [SIG API Machinery, Cloud Provider, Scalability and Testing]\n3537: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\n3538: - Improve error message when applying CRDs before the CRD exists in a cluster ([#107363](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107363), [@eddiezane](https://github.com/eddiezane)) [SIG CLI]\n3539: ",
            "endLine": 3539,
            "startLine": 3536
          },
          {
            "content": "2301:   JobTrackingWithFinalizers is still disabled by default. ([#109486](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109486), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor)) [SIG Apps and Testing]\n2302: - Kubeadm: only taint control plane nodes when the legacy \"master\" taint is present. This avoids a bug where \"kubeadm upgrade\" will re-taint a control plane node with the new \"control plane\" taint even if the user explicitly untainted the node. ([#109841]( ... [line truncated]\n2303: \n2304: ",
            "endLine": 2304,
            "startLine": 2301
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3769: \n3770: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\n3771:   - `--port`\n3772: ",
            "endLine": 3772,
            "startLine": 3769
          },
          {
            "content": "2236: \n2237: - Controller-manager: the following flags have no effect and would be removed in v1.24:\n2238:   - `--port`\n2239: ",
            "endLine": 2239,
            "startLine": 2236
          },
          {
            "content": "1771: \n1772: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106132, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\n1773: - Fix a bug that `--disabled-metrics` doesn't function well. (#105793, @Huang-Wei) [SIG API Machinery, Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]\n1774: ",
            "endLine": 1774,
            "startLine": 1771
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1421: - Migrated `pod-security-admission` to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#114471](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/114471), [@Namanl2001](https://github.com/Namanl2 ... [line truncated]\n1422: - Migrated controller functions to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116930](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116930), [@fatsheep9146](https://github.com/fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\n1423: - Migrated the Job controller (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116910](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116910), [@fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\n1424: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 1427,
            "startLine": 1421
          },
          {
            "content": "2264: - Kubernetes is now built with Go 1.20.6 ([#119324](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/119324), [@xmudrii](https://github.com/xmudrii)) [SIG API Machinery, Auth, Cloud Provider, Release and Testing]\n2265: - Migrate `pkg/controller/endpoint` to contextual logging ([#116755](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116755), [@my-git9](https://github.com/my-git9)) [SIG Apps, Instrumentation and Network]\n2266: - Migrated the `EndpointSlice` and `EndpointSliceMirroring` controllers (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#115295](https://github.com/kuber ... [line truncated]\n2267: - Move non-graceful node shutdown to GA. ([#118228](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118228), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory)) [SIG Apps, Storage and Testing]\n2268: ",
            "endLine": 2268,
            "startLine": 2264
          },
          {
            "content": "1682: - `kube-apiserver`: Improved memory use when performing `GetList` on the cache. ([#116327](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116327), [@sxllwx](https://github.com/sxllwx))\n1683: - `kube-controller-manager` and `cloud-controller-manager` have changed the\n1684:   name of controllers that can be turned on/off that are passed to the `--controllers`\n1685:   flag (e.g., `pod-garbage-collector-controller`). The old names (eg `podgc`) are\n1686:   also accepted and aliased to the new names. ([#115813](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/115813), [@atiratree](https://github.com/atiratree))\n1687: ",
            "endLine": 1687,
            "startLine": 1682
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md"
      }
    ]
  }
}

range node.Status.Conditions {\\n616: \\t\\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\\n617: \\t\\t\\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\\n618: \\t\\t}\\n619: \",\"endLine\":619,\"startLine\":612},{\"content\":\"532: \\n533: func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\\n534: \\toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\\n535: \\tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\\n536: \\n537: \\t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\\n538: \\t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\\n539: \\tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\\n540: \\t\\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\\n541: \\t\\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\\n542: \\t}\\n543: \",\"endLine\":543,\"startLine\":532},{\"content\":\"243: \\n244: \\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is an artificial delay added to all service syncs triggered by pod changes.\\n245: \\t// This can be used to reduce overall number of all endpoint slice updates.\\n246: \\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\\n247: \\n248: \\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\\n249: \\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\\n250: \\n5040: // NodeConditionType defines node's condition\\n5041: type NodeConditionType string\\n5042: \\n5043: // These are valid but not exhaustive conditions of node. A cloud provider may set a condition not listed here.\\n5044: // Relevant events contain \\\"NodeReady\\\", \\\"NodeNotReady\\\", \\\"NodeSchedulable\\\", and \\\"NodeNotSchedulable\\\".\\n5045: const (\\n5046: \\t// NodeReady means kubelet is healthy and ready to accept pods.\\n5047: \\tNodeReady NodeConditionType = \\\"Ready\\\"\\n5048: \\t// NodeMemoryPressure means the kubelet is under pressure due to insufficient available memory.\\n5049: \\tNodeMemoryPressure NodeConditionType = \\\"MemoryPressure\\\"\\n5050: \\t// NodeDiskPressure means the kubelet is under pressure due to insufficient available disk.\\n5051: \\tNodeDiskPressure NodeConditionType = \\\"DiskPressure\\\"\\n5052: \\t// NodeNetworkUnavailable means that network for the node is not correctly configured.\\n5053: \\tNodeNetworkUnavailable NodeConditionType = \\\"NetworkUnavailable\\\"\\n5054: )\\n5055: \",\"endLine\":5055,\"startLine\":5039},{\"content\":\"5055: \\n5056: // NodeCondition represents the node's         "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "612: \n613: // isNodeReady returns true if a node is ready; false otherwise.\n614: func isNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\n615: \tfor _, c := range node.Status.Conditions {\n616: \t\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\n617: \t\t\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n618: \t\t}\n619: ",
            "endLine": 619,
            "startLine": 612
          },
          {
            "content": "532: \n533: func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\n534: \toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\n535: \tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\n536: \n537: \t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\n538: \t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\n539: \tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\n540: \t\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\n541: \t\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n542: \t}\n543: ",
            "endLine": 543,
            "startLine": 532
          },
          {
            "content": "243: \n244: \t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is an artificial delay added to all service syncs triggered by pod changes.\n245: \t// This can be used to reduce overall number of all endpoint slice updates.\n246: \tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\n247: \n248: \t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n249: \t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n250: ",
            "endLine": 250,
            "startLine": 243
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "127: \n128: \tif address.NodeName != nil {\n129: \t\tendpoint.NodeName = address.NodeName\n130: \t}\n131: ",
            "endLine": 131,
            "startLine": 127
          },
          {
            "content": "116: \n117: // addressToEndpoint converts an address from an Endpoints resource to an\n118: // EndpointSlice endpoint.\n119: func addressToEndpoint(address corev1.EndpointAddress, ready bool) *discovery.Endpoint {\n120: \tendpoint := &discovery.Endpoint{\n121: \t\tAddresses: []string{address.IP},\n122: \t\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\n123: \t\t\tReady: &ready,\n124: \t\t},\n125: ",
            "endLine": 125,
            "startLine": 116
          },
          {
            "content": "16: \n17: package endpointslicemirroring\n18: \n19: ",
            "endLine": 19,
            "startLine": 16
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/controller/endpointslicemirroring/utils.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/controller/endpointslicemirroring/utils.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "133: \n134: // EndpointConditions represents the current condition of an endpoint.\n135: type EndpointConditions struct {\n136: \t// ready indicates that this endpoint is prepared to receive traffic,\n137: \t// according to whatever system is managing the endpoint. A nil value\n138: \t// indicates an unknown state. In most cases consumers should interpret this\n139: \t// unknown state as ready. For compatibility reasons, ready should never be\n140: \t// \"true\" for terminating endpoints.\n141: \t// +optional\n142: \tReady *bool `json:\"ready,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=ready\"`\n143: \n144: \t// serving is identical to ready except that it is set regardless of the\n145: \t// terminating state of endpoints. This condition should be set to true for\n146: \t// a ready endpoint that is terminating. If nil, consumers should defer to\n147: \t// the ready condition.\n148: \t// +optional\n149: ",
            "endLine": 149,
            "startLine": 133
          },
          {
            "content": "89: \n90: \t// conditions contains information about the current status of the endpoint.\n91: \tConditions EndpointConditions `json:\"conditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=conditions\"`\n92: \n93: \t// hostname of this endpoint. This field may be used by consumers of\n94: \t// endpoints to distinguish endpoints from each other (e.g. in DNS names).\n95: \t// Multiple endpoints which use the same hostname should be considered\n96: \t// fungible (e.g. multiple A values in DNS). Must be lowercase and pass DNS\n97: ",
            "endLine": 97,
            "startLine": 89
          },
          {
            "content": "121: \n122: \t// nodeName represents the name of the Node hosting this endpoint. This can\n123: \t// be used to determine endpoints local to a Node.\n124: \t// +optional\n125: \tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n126: \n127: \t// hints contains information associated with how an endpoint should be\n128: \t// consumed.\n129: ",
            "endLine": 129,
            "startLine": 121
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/api/discovery/v1beta1/types.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",

            "content": "126: \n127: // EndpointConditions represents the current condition of an endpoint.\n128: type EndpointConditions struct {\n129: \t// ready indicates that this endpoint is prepared to receive traffic,\n130: \t// according to whatever system is managing the endpoint. This field may be used by consumers of\n91: \t// endpoints to distinguish endpoints from each other (e.g. in DNS names).\n92: \t// Multiple endpoints which use the same hostname should be considered\n93: \t// fungible (e.g. multiple A values in DNS).           {
            "content": "108: \n109: // EndpointConditions represents the current condition of an endpoint.\n110: type EndpointConditions struct {\n111: \t// ready indicates that this endpoint is prepared to receive traffic,\n112: \t// according to whatever system is managing the endpoint. A nil value\n113: \t// indicates an unknown state. In most cases consumers should interpret this\n114: \t// unknown state as ready. For compatibility reasons, ready should never be\n115: \t// \"true\" for terminating endpoints, except when the normal readiness\n116: \t// behavior is being explicitly overridden, for example when the associated\n117: \t// Service has set the publishNotReadyAddresses flag.\n118: \tReady *bool\n119: \n120: \t// serving is identical to ready except that it is set regardless of the\n121: \t// terminating state of endpoints. This condition should be set to true for\n122: \t// a ready endpoint that is terminating. If nil, consumers should defer to\n123: \t// the ready condition.\n124: \t// +optional\n125: ",
            "endLine": 125,
            "startLine": 108

\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\t\\\"golang.org/x/time/rate\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n27: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n28: \\tapierrors \\tdiscoveryinformers \\\"k8s.io/client-go/informers/discovery/v1\\\"\\n35: \\tclientset \\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\\\"\\n37: \\tv1core \\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/core/v1\\\"\\n38: \\tcorelisters \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\\\"\\n39: \\tdiscoverylisters \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/discovery/v1\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue\\\"\\n43: \\tendpointslicerec \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice\\\"\\n44: \\tendpointslicemetrics \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\\\"\\n46: \\tendpointsliceutil \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\\\"\\n47: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n48: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller\\\"\\n49: \\tendpointslicepkg \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/util/endpointslice\\\"\\n50: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n51: )\\n52: \\n53: const (\\n54: \\t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is\\n55: \\t// dropped out of the queue. Any sync error, such as a failure to create or\\n56: \\t// update an EndpointSlice could trigger a retry. With the current\\n57: \\t// rate-limiter in use (1s*2^(numRetries-1)) the following numbers represent\\n58: \\t// the sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\\n59: \\t//\\n60: \\t// 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 64s, 128s, 256s, 512s, 1000s (max)\\n61: \\tmaxRetries = 15\\n62: \\n63: \\t// endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay indicates the minimum delay before\\n64: \\t// queuing a syncService call after an EndpointSlice changes. If\\n65: \\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is greater than this value, it will be used\\n66: \\t// instead. \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n51: )\n52: \n53: const (\n54: \t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is\n55: \t// dropped out of the queue. Any sync error, such as a failure to create or\n56: \t// update an EndpointSlice could trigger a retry. With the current\n57: \t// rate-limiter in use (1s*2^(numRetries-1)) the following numbers represent\n58: \t// the sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\n59: \t//\n60: \t// 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 64s, 128s, 256s, 512s, 1000s (max)\n61: \tmaxRetries = 15\n62: \n63: \t// endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay indicates the minimum delay before\n64: \t// queuing a syncService call after an EndpointSlice changes. If\n65: \t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is greater than this value, it will be used\n66: \t// instead. This helps batch processing of changes to multiple\n67: \t// EndpointSlices.\n68: \tendpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay = 1 * time.Second\n69: \n70: \t// defaultSyncBackOff is the default backoff period for syncService calls.\n71: \tdefaultSyncBackOff = 1 * time.Second\n72: \t// maxSyncBackOff is the max backoff period for syncService calls.\n73: \tmaxSyncBackOff = 1000 * time.Second\n74: \n75: \t// controllerName is a unique value used with LabelManagedBy to indicated\n76: \t// the component managing an EndpointSlice.\n77: \tcontrollerName = \"endpointslice-controller.k8s.io\"\n78: )\n79: \n80: // NewController creates and initializes a new Controller\n81: func NewController(ctx context.Context, podInformer coreinformers.PodInformer,\n82: \tserviceInformer coreinformers.ServiceInformer,\n83: \tnodeInformer coreinformers.NodeInformer,\n84: \tendpointSliceInformer discoveryinformers.EndpointSliceInformer,\n85: \tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32,\n86: \tclient clientset.Interface,\n87: \tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration,\n88: ) *Controller {\n89: \tbroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster(record.WithContext(ctx))\n90: \trecorder := broadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, v1.EventSource{Component: \"endpoint-slice-controller\"})\n91: \n92: \tendpointslicemetrics.RegisterMetrics()\n93: \n94: \tc := &Controller{\n95: \t\tclient: client,\n96: \t\t// This is similar to the DefaultControllerRateLimiter, just with a\n97: \t\t// significantly higher default backoff (1s vs 5ms). This controller\n98: \t\t// processes events that can require significant EndpointSlice changes,\n99: \t\t// such as an update to a Service or Deployment. leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the             "content": "207: \t\t}\n208: \t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n209: \t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n210: \t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n211: \t\t}\n212: ",

          },
          {
            "content": "165: \n166: \t// Build data structures for desired state.\n167: \tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\n168: \tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n169: \n170: \tfor _, pod := range pods {\n171: \t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\n172: \t\t\tcontinue\n173: ",
            "endLine": 173,
            "startLine": 165
          },
          {
            "content": "194: \t\t\t}\n195: \t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n196: \t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n197: \t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n198: \t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n199: \t\t\t// stuck forever.\n200: \t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n201: \t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n202: \t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n203: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n204: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n205: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n206: ",
            "endLine": 206,
            "startLine": 194
          }
        ],

            "content": "35: \n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n39: \tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n40: \t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n41: \t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\n42: \tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\n43: \tep := discovery.Endpoint{\n44: \t\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\n45: \t\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\n46: \t\t\tReady:       &ready,\n47: \t\t\tServing:     &serving,\n48: ",
