            "content": "404: \n405: \t// We call ComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime here to make sure that the\n406: \t// state of the trigger time tracker gets updated even if the sync turns out\n407: \t// to be no-op and we don't update the endpoints object.\n408: \tendpointsLastChangeTriggerTime := e.triggerTimeTracker.\n409: \t\tComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime(namespace, service, pods)\n410: \n411: \tsubsets := []v1.EndpointSubset{}\n412: \tvar totalReadyEps int\n413: \tvar totalNotReadyEps int\n414: \n415: \tfor _, pod := range pods {\n416: \t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses) {\n417: \t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Pod is not included on endpoints for Service\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service))\n418: \t\t\tcontinue\n419: ",
            "endLine": 419,
            "startLine": 404
          },
          {
            "content": "206: \n207: func podToEndpointAddressForService(svc *v1.Service, pod *v1.Pod) (*v1.EndpointAddress, error) {\n208: \tvar endpointIP string\n209: \tipFamily := v1.IPv4Protocol\n210: ",
            "endLine": 210,

          }
        ],

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "825: \n826: func (j *TestJig) waitForPodsReady(ctx context.Context, pods []string) error {\n827: \ttimeout := 2 * time.Minute\n828: \tif !e2epod.CheckPodsRunningReady(ctx, j.Client, j.Namespace, pods, timeout) {\n829: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"timeout waiting for %d pods to be ready\", len(pods))\n830: \t}\n831: ",
            "endLine": 831,
            "startLine": 825
          },
          {
            "content": "283: \n284: // ListNodesWithEndpoint returns a list of nodes on which the\n285: // endpoints of the given Service are running.\n286: func (j *TestJig) ListNodesWithEndpoint(ctx context.Context) ([]v1.Node, error) {\n287: \tnodeNames, err := j.GetEndpointNodeNames(ctx)\n288: \tif err != nil {\n289: ",
            "endLine": 289,
            "startLine": 283
          },
          {
            "content": "328: \n329: // WaitForEndpointOnNode waits for a service endpoint on the given node.\n330: func (j *TestJig) WaitForEndpointOnNode(ctx context.Context, nodeName string) error {\n331: \treturn wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, framework.Poll, KubeProxyLagTimeout, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n332: \t\tendpoints, err := j.Client.CoreV1().Endpoints(j.Namespace).Get(ctx, j.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})\n333: \t\tif err != nil {\n334: \t\t\tframework.Logf(\"Get endpoints for service %s/%s failed (%s)\", j.Namespace, j.Name, err)\n335: \t\t\treturn false, nil\n336: ",
            "endLine": 336,

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package endpointslice\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n25: \\tapiequality \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\\\"\\n26: \\tapimachineryvalidation \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\\\"\\n27: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n31: \\tendpointutil \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n33: \\tutilnet \\\"k8s.io/utils/net\\\"\\n34: )\\n35: \\n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\\n38: \\tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\\n39: \\tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\\n40: \\t// For compatibility reasons, \\\"ready\\\" should never be \\\"true\\\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\\n41: \\t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\\n42: \\tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\\n43: \\tep := discovery.Endpoint{\\n44: \\t\\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\\n45: \\t\\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\\n46: \\t\\t\\tReady:       &ready,\\n47: \\t\\t\\tServing:     &serving,\\n48: \\t\\t\\tTerminating: &terminating,\\n49: \\t\\t},\\n50: \\t\\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\\n51: \\t\\t\\tKind:      \\\"Pod\\\",\\n52: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\\n53: \\t\\t\\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\\n54: \\t\\t\\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\\n55: \\t\\t},\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \\\"\\\" {\\n59: \\t\\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\\n60: \\t}\\n61: \\n62: \\tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \\\"\\\" {\\n63: \\t\\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\\n64: \\t\\tep.Zone = &zone\\n65: \\t}\\n66: \\n67: \\tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\\n68: \\t\\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\\n69: \\t}\\n70: \\n71: \\treturn ep\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // getEndpointPorts returns a list of EndpointPorts generated from a Service\\n75: // and Pod.\\n76: func getEndpointPorts(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, pod *v1.Pod) []discovery.EndpointPort {\\n77: \\tendpointPorts := []discovery.EndpointPort{}\\n78: \\n79: \\t// Allow headless service not to have ports.\\n80: \\tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\\n81: \\t\\treturn endpointPorts\\n82: \\t}\\n83: \\n84: \\tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\\n85: \\t\\tservicePort := &service.Spec.Ports[i]\\n86: \\n87: \\t\\tportName := servicePort.Name\\n88: \\t\\tportProto := servicePort.Protocol\\n89: \\t\\tportNum, err := findPort(pod, servicePort)\\n90: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n91: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Failed to find port for service\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"err\\\", err)\\n92: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n93: \\t\\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\t\\ti32PortNum := int32(portNum)\\n96: \\t\\tendpointPorts = append(endpointPorts, discovery.EndpointPort{\\n97: \\t\\t\\tName:        &portName,\\n98: \\t\\t\\tPort:        &i32PortNum,\\n99: \\t\\t\\tProtocol:    &portProto,\\n100: \\t\\t\\tAppProtocol: servicePort.AppProtocol,\\n101: \\t\\t})\\n102: \\t}\\n103: \\n104: \\treturn endpointPorts\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: // getEndpointAddresses returns a list of addresses generated from a pod status.\\n108: func getEndpointAddresses(podStatus v1.PodStatus, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) []string {\\n109: \\taddresses := []string{}\\n110: \\n111: \\tfor _, podIP := range podStatus.PodIPs {\\n112: \\t\\tisIPv6PodIP := utilnet.IsIPv6String(podIP.IP)\\n113: \\t\\tif isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv6 {\\n114: \\t\\t\\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\\n115: \\t\\t}\\n116: \\n117: \\t\\tif !isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv4 {\\n118: \\t\\t\\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\\n119: \\t\\t}\\n120: \\t}\\n121: \\n122: \\treturn addresses\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: // newEndpointSlice returns an EndpointSlice generated from a service and\\n126: // endpointMeta.\\n127: func newEndpointSlice(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, endpointMeta *endpointMeta, controllerName string) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n128: \\tgvk := schema.GroupVersionKind{Version: \\\"v1\\\", Kind: \\\"Service\\\"}\\n129: \\townerRef := metav1.NewControllerRef(service, gvk)\\n130: \\tepSlice := &discovery.EndpointSlice{\\n131: \\t\\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\\n132: \\t\\t\\tLabels:          map[string]string{},\\n133: \\t\\t\\tGenerateName:    getEndpointSlicePrefix(service.Name),\\n134: \\t\\t\\tOwnerReferences: []metav1.OwnerReference{*ownerRef},\\n135: \\t\\t\\tNamespace:       service.Namespace,\\n136: \\t\\t},\\n137: \\t\\tPorts:       endpointMeta.ports,\\n138: \\t\\tAddressType: endpointMeta.addressType,\\n139: \\t\\tEndpoints:   []discovery.Endpoint{},\\n140: \\t}\\n141: \\t// add parent service labels\\n142: \\tepSlice.Labels, _ = setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, epSlice, service, controllerName)\\n143: \\n144: \\treturn epSlice\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: // getEndpointSlicePrefix returns a suitable prefix for an EndpointSlice name.\\n148: func getEndpointSlicePrefix(serviceName string) string {\\n149: \\t// use the dash (if the name isn't too long) to make the pod name a bit prettier\\n150: \\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s-\\\", serviceName)\\n151: \\tif len(apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain(prefix, true)) != 0 {\\n152: \\t\\tprefix = serviceName\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn prefix\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // ownedBy returns true if the provided EndpointSlice is owned by the provided\\n158: // Service.\\n159: func ownedBy(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, svc *v1.Service) bool {\\n160: \\tfor _, o := range endpointSlice.OwnerReferences {\\n161: \\t\\tif o.UID == svc.UID && o.Kind == \\\"Service\\\" && o.APIVersion == \\\"v1\\\" {\\n162: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t}\\n165: \\treturn false\\n166: }\\n167: \\n168: // getSliceToFill will return the EndpointSlice that will be closest to full\\n169: // when numEndpoints are added. If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\\n170: // will be returned.\\n171: func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\\n172: \\tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\\n173: \\tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\\n174: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\\n175: \\t\\tcurrentDiff := maxEndpoints - (numEndpoints + len(endpointSlice.Endpoints))\\n176: \\t\\tif currentDiff >= 0 && currentDiff < closestDiff {\\n177: \\t\\t\\tclosestDiff = currentDiff\\n178: \\t\\t\\tclosestSlice = endpointSlice\\n179: \\t\\t\\tif closestDiff == 0 {\\n180: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn closestSlice\\n181: \\t\\t\\t}\\n182: \\t\\t}\\n183: \\t}\\n184: \\treturn closestSlice\\n185: }\\n186: \\n187: // addTriggerTimeAnnotation adds a triggerTime annotation to an EndpointSlice\\n188: func addTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) {\\n189: \\tif endpointSlice.Annotations == nil {\\n190: \\t\\tendpointSlice.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\\n191: \\t}\\n192: \\n193: \\tif !triggerTime.IsZero() {\\n194: \\t\\tendpointSlice.Annotations[v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime] = triggerTime.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\\n195: \\t} else { // No new trigger time, clear the annotation.\\n196: \\t\\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\\n197: \\t}\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\\n201: // an EndpointSlice.\\n202: func ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) (string, error) {\\n203: \\tif endpointSlice == nil {\\n204: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"nil EndpointSlice passed to ServiceControllerKey()\\\")\\n205: \\t}\\n206: \\tserviceName, ok := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\\n207: \\tif !ok || serviceName == \\\"\\\" {\\n208: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"EndpointSlice missing %s label\\\", discovery.LabelServiceName)\\n209: \\t}\\n210: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s/%s\\\", endpointSlice.Namespace, serviceName), nil\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\\n217: \\tupdated := false\\n218: \\tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n219: \\tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n220: \\n221: \\t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\\n222: \\t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\\n223: \\tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\\n224: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n225: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n226: \\t\\t}\\n227: \\t\\t// copy endpoint slice labels\\n228: \\t\\tepLabels[key] = value\\n229: \\t}\\n230: \\n231: \\tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\\n232: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n233: \\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Service using reserved endpoint slices label\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"skipping\\\", key, \\\"label\\\", value)\\n234: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n235: \\t\\t}\\n236: \\t\\t// copy service labels\\n237: \\t\\tsvcLabels[key] = value\\n238: \\t}\\n239: \\n240: \\t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the corresponding service labels\\n241: \\tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\\n242: \\t\\tupdated = true\\n243: \\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\\n246: \\tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\\n247: \\t\\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \\\"\\\"\\n248: \\t} else {\\n249: \\t\\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\\n250: \\t}\\n251: \\n252: \\t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\\n253: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\\n254: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\\n255: \\n256: \\treturn svcLabels, updated\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\\n261: \\tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\\n262: \\t\\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\\n263: \\t\\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\\n264: \\t\\treturn true\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\treturn false\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\\n270: // endpoints they contain.\\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\\n272: \\n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\\n276: \\treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\\n277: }\\n278: \\n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\\n281: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\\n282: \\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\\n283: \\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\\n284: \\t// v1.IPFamily types\\n285: \\n286: \\t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\\n287: \\t// as it gets deprecated\\n288: \\tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\\n289: \\t\\tif family == v1.IPv4Protocol {\\n290: \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n291: \\t\\t}\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\tif family == v1.IPv6Protocol {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\\n299: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\\n303: \\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\\n304: \\n305: \\t// Why do we need this:\\n306: \\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\\n307: \\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\\n308: \\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\\n309: \\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\\n310: \\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\\n311: \\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\\n312: \\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\\n313: \\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\\n314: \\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\\n315: \\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\\n316: \\n317: \\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\\n318: \\t\\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\\n319: \\t\\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\\n320: \\t\\t\\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\\n323: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"addressType\\\", addrType, \\\"clusterIP\\\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\\n324: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\t// headless\\n328: \\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\\n329: \\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\\n330: \\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\\n331: \\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\\n332: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n333: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n334: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service))\\n335: \\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n339: \\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\\n340: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n341: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\\n344: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n347: \\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\\n348: \\t\\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\\n349: \\t\\t\\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\treturn unchangedSlices\\n354: }\\n355: \\n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\\n358: // with a value set to \\\"Auto\\\" or \\\"auto\\\". When both are set,\\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\\n361: \\tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\\n362: \\tif !ok {\\n363: \\t\\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\\n364: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n366: \\t\\t}\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn val == \\\"Auto\\\" || val == \\\"auto\\\"\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\\n375: \\treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \\\"\\\"\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\\n379: // targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\\n380: // string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\\n381: // match is found, fail.\\n382: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n383: func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\\n384: \\tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\\n385: \\tswitch portName.Type {\\n386: \\tcase intstr.String:\\n387: \\t\\tname := portName.StrVal\\n388: \\t\\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n389: \\t\\t\\tfor _, port := range container.Ports {\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\tif port.Name == name && port.Protocol == svcPort.Protocol {\\n391: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn int(port.ContainerPort), nil\\n392: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n393: \\t\\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\t}\\n395: \\tcase intstr.Int:\\n396: \\t\\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\\\", pod.UID)\\n400: }\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package endpointslice\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"fmt\"\n21: \t\"time\"\n22: \n23: \tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n24: \tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n25: \tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n26: \tapimachineryvalidation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\"\n27: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n28: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n31: \tendpointutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n33: \tutilnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n34: )\n35: \n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n39: \tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n40: \t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n41: \t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\n42: \tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\n43: \tep := discovery.Endpoint{\n44: \t\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\n45: \t\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\n46: \t\t\tReady:       &ready,\n47: \t\t\tServing:     &serving,\n48: \t\t\tTerminating: &terminating,\n49: \t\t},\n50: \t\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\n51: \t\t\tKind:      \"Pod\",\n52: \t\t\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\n53: \t\t\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\n54: \t\t\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\n55: \t\t},\n56: \t}\n57: \n58: \tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" {\n59: \t\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\n60: \t}\n61: \n62: \tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \"\" {\n63: \t\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\n64: \t\tep.Zone = &zone\n65: \t}\n66: \n67: \tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\n68: \t\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\n69: \t}\n70: \n71: \treturn ep\n72: }\n73: \n74: // getEndpointPorts returns a list of EndpointPorts generated from a Service\n75: // and Pod.\n76: func getEndpointPorts(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, pod *v1.Pod) []discovery.EndpointPort {\n77: \tendpointPorts := []discovery.EndpointPort{}\n78: \n79: \t// Allow headless service not to have ports.\n80: \tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\n81: \t\treturn endpointPorts\n82: \t}\n83: \n84: \tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\n85: \t\tservicePort := &service.Spec.Ports[i]\n86: \n87: \t\tportName := servicePort.Name\n88: \t\tportProto := servicePort.Protocol\n89: \t\tportNum, err := findPort(pod, servicePort)\n90: \t\tif err != nil {\n91: \t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Failed to find port for service\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"err\", err)\n92: \t\t\tcontinue\n93: \t\t}\n94: \n95: \t\ti32PortNum := int32(portNum)\n96: \t\tendpointPorts = append(endpointPorts, discovery.EndpointPort{\n97: \t\t\tName:        &portName,\n98: \t\t\tPort:        &i32PortNum,\n99: \t\t\tProtocol:    &portProto,\n100: \t\t\tAppProtocol: servicePort.AppProtocol,\n101: \t\t})\n102: \t}\n103: \n104: \treturn endpointPorts\n105: }\n106: \n107: // getEndpointAddresses returns a list of addresses generated from a pod status.\n108: func getEndpointAddresses(podStatus v1.PodStatus, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) []string {\n109: \taddresses := []string{}\n110: \n111: \tfor _, podIP := range podStatus.PodIPs {\n112: \t\tisIPv6PodIP := utilnet.IsIPv6String(podIP.IP)\n113: \t\tif isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv6 {\n114: \t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n115: \t\t}\n116: \n117: \t\tif !isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv4 {\n118: \t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n119: \t\t}\n120: \t}\n121: \n122: \treturn addresses\n123: }\n124: \n125: // newEndpointSlice returns an EndpointSlice generated from a service and\n126: // endpointMeta.\n127: func newEndpointSlice(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, endpointMeta *endpointMeta, controllerName string) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n128: \tgvk := schema.GroupVersionKind{Version: \"v1\", Kind: \"Service\"}\n129: \townerRef := metav1.NewControllerRef(service, gvk)\n130: \tepSlice := &discovery.EndpointSlice{\n131: \t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n132: \t\t\tLabels:          map[string]string{},\n133: \t\t\tGenerateName:    getEndpointSlicePrefix(service.Name),\n134: \t\t\tOwnerReferences: []metav1.OwnerReference{*ownerRef},\n135: \t\t\tNamespace:       service.Namespace,\n136: \t\t},\n137: \t\tPorts:       endpointMeta.ports,\n138: \t\tAddressType: endpointMeta.addressType,\n139: \t\tEndpoints:   []discovery.Endpoint{},\n140: \t}\n141: \t// add parent service labels\n142: \tepSlice.Labels, _ = setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, epSlice, service, controllerName)\n143: \n144: \treturn epSlice\n145: }\n146: \n147: // getEndpointSlicePrefix returns a suitable prefix for an EndpointSlice name.\n148: func getEndpointSlicePrefix(serviceName string) string {\n149: \t// use the dash (if the name isn't too long) to make the pod name a bit prettier\n150: \tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-\", serviceName)\n151: \tif len(apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain(prefix, true)) != 0 {\n152: \t\tprefix = serviceName\n153: \t}\n154: \treturn prefix\n155: }\n156: \n157: // ownedBy returns true if the provided EndpointSlice is owned by the provided\n158: // Service.\n159: func ownedBy(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n160: \tfor _, o := range endpointSlice.OwnerReferences {\n161: \t\tif o.UID == svc.UID && o.Kind == \"Service\" && o.APIVersion == \"v1\" {\n162: \t\t\treturn true\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t}\n165: \treturn false\n166: }\n167: \n168: // getSliceToFill will return the EndpointSlice that will be closest to full\n169: // when numEndpoints are added. If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\n170: // will be returned.\n171: func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n172: \tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\n173: \tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\n174: \tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n175: \t\tcurrentDiff := maxEndpoints - (numEndpoints + len(endpointSlice.Endpoints))\n176: \t\tif currentDiff >= 0 && currentDiff < closestDiff {\n177: \t\t\tclosestDiff = currentDiff\n178: \t\t\tclosestSlice = endpointSlice\n179: \t\t\tif closestDiff == 0 {\n180: \t\t\t\treturn closestSlice\n181: \t\t\t}\n182: \t\t}\n183: \t}\n184: \treturn closestSlice\n185: }\n186: \n187: // addTriggerTimeAnnotation adds a triggerTime annotation to an EndpointSlice\n188: func addTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) {\n189: \tif endpointSlice.Annotations == nil {\n190: \t\tendpointSlice.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n191: \t}\n192: \n193: \tif !triggerTime.IsZero() {\n194: \t\tendpointSlice.Annotations[v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime] = triggerTime.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n195: \t} else { // No new trigger time, clear the annotation.\n196: \t\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\n197: \t}\n198: }\n199: \n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\n201: // an EndpointSlice.\n202: func ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) (string, error) {\n203: \tif endpointSlice == nil {\n204: \t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"nil EndpointSlice passed to ServiceControllerKey()\")\n205: \t}\n206: \tserviceName, ok := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n207: \tif !ok || serviceName == \"\" {\n208: \t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"EndpointSlice missing %s label\", discovery.LabelServiceName)\n209: \t}\n210: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", endpointSlice.Namespace, serviceName), nil\n211: }\n212: \n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\n217: \tupdated := false\n218: \tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\n219: \tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\n220: \n221: \t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\n222: \t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\n223: \tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\n224: \t\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\n225: \t\t\tcontinue\n226: \t\t}\n227: \t\t// copy endpoint slice labels\n228: \t\tepLabels[key] = value\n229: \t}\n230: \n231: \tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\n232: \t\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\n233: \t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Service using reserved endpoint slices label\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"skipping\", key, \"label\", value)\n234: \t\t\tcontinue\n235: \t\t}\n236: \t\t// copy service labels\n237: \t\tsvcLabels[key] = value\n238: \t}\n239: \n240: \t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the corresponding service labels\n241: \tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\n242: \t\tupdated = true\n243: \t}\n244: \n245: \t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\n246: \tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\n247: \t\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \"\"\n248: \t} else {\n249: \t\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\n250: \t}\n251: \n252: \t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\n253: \tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\n254: \tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\n255: \n256: \treturn svcLabels, updated\n257: }\n258: \n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\n261: \tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\n262: \t\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\n263: \t\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\n264: \t\treturn true\n265: \t}\n266: \treturn false\n267: }\n268: \n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\n270: // endpoints they contain.\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\n272: \n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\n276: \treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\n277: }\n278: \n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\n281: \tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\n282: \t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\n283: \t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\n284: \t// v1.IPFamily types\n285: \n286: \t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\n287: \t// as it gets deprecated\n288: \tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\n289: \t\tif family == v1.IPv4Protocol {\n290: \t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\n291: \t\t}\n292: \n293: \t\tif family == v1.IPv6Protocol {\n294: \t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t}\n297: \n298: \tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\n299: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\n300: \t}\n301: \n302: \t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\n303: \t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\n304: \n305: \t// Why do we need this:\n306: \t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\n307: \t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\n308: \t// if the controller manager connected to old api\n309: \t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\n310: \t// removing all slices already created for this service.\n311: \t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\n312: \t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\n313: \t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\n314: \t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\n315: \t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\n316: \n317: \tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\n318: \t\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\n319: \t\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\n320: \t\t\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\n321: \t\t}\n322: \t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\n323: \t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"addressType\", addrType, \"clusterIP\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\n324: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n325: \t}\n326: \n327: \t// headless\n328: \t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\n329: \t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\n330: \t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\n331: \t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\n332: \tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\n333: \tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n334: \tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service))\n335: \treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n336: }\n337: \n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\n339: \tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\n340: \tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\n341: \t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n342: \t}\n343: \tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\n344: \t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n345: \t}\n346: \tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n347: \tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\n348: \t\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\n349: \t\t\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\n350: \t\t}\n351: \t}\n352: \n353: \treturn unchangedSlices\n354: }\n355: \n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\n358: // with a value set to \"Auto\" or \"auto\". When both are set,\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\n361: \tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\n362: \tif !ok {\n363: \t\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\n364: \t\tif !ok {\n365: \t\t\treturn false\n366: \t\t}\n367: \t}\n368: \treturn val == \"Auto\" || val == \"auto\"\n369: }\n370: \n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\n375: \treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \"\"\n376: }\n377: \n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\n379: // targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\n380: // string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\n381: // match is found, fail.\n382: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n383: func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\n384: \tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\n385: \tswitch portName.Type {\n386: \tcase intstr.String:\n387: \t\tname := portName.StrVal\n388: \t\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n389: \t\t\tfor _, port := range container.Ports {\n390: \t\t\t\tif port.Name == name && port.Protocol == svcPort.Protocol {\n391: \t\t\t\t\treturn int(port.ContainerPort), nil\n392: \t\t\t\t}\n393: \t\t\t}\n394: \t\t}\n395: \tcase intstr.Int:\n396: \t\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\n397: \t}\n398: \n399: \treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\", pod.UID)\n400: }\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go"
  }
}
23. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package endpointslice\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\tcorev1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n26: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n28: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n31: \\tutilerrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n33: \\tclientset \\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\\\"\\n34: \\tcorelisters \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\\\"\\n39: \\tendpointsliceutil \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n41: )\\n42: \\n43: // Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\\n44: // desired state\\n45: type Reconciler struct {\\n46: \\tclient               clientset.Interface\\n47: \\tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\\n48: \\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\\n49: \\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\\n50: \\tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\\n51: \\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\\n52: \\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\\n53: \\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\\n54: \\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\\n55: \\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\\n56: \\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\\n57: \\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\\n58: \\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\\n59: \\tcontrollerName string\\n60: }\\n61: \\n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\\n63: \\n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\\n67: \\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\\n68: \\t\\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\\n69: \\t}\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\\n73: // that logic in Reconciler\\n74: type endpointMeta struct {\\n75: \\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\\n76: \\taddressType discovery.AddressType\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\\n84: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\\n85: \\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\\n86: \\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\\n87: \\n88: \\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\\n89: \\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\\n90: \\n91: \\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\\n92: \\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\\n93: \\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\\n94: \\t// for further adjustment\\n95: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n96: \\t\\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\\n97: \\t\\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\\n98: \\t\\t\\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\\n100: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n101: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\\\", \\\"existingSlice\\\", existingSlice, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n102: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n103: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t\\t}\\n106: \\n107: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n108: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n109: \\t\\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\t\\t// add list if it is not on our map\\n112: \\t\\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\\n113: \\t\\t\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\\n114: \\t\\t}\\n115: \\n116: \\t\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\\n117: \\t}\\n118: \\n119: \\t// reconcile for existing.\\n120: \\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\\n121: \\t\\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\\n122: \\t\\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\\n123: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n124: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, err)\\n125: \\t\\t}\\n126: \\t}\\n127: \\n128: \\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\\n129: \\t// by the service\\n130: \\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\\n131: \\t\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\n132: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n133: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\\\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\\n134: \\t\\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\\n136: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"delete\\\").Inc()\\n137: \\t\\t}\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\\n148: \\terrs := []error{}\\n149: \\n150: \\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n151: \\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n152: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n153: \\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\\n154: \\n155: \\t// Build data structures for existing state.\\n156: \\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n157: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n158: \\t\\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\\n159: \\t\\t\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\\n160: \\t\\t\\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\\n161: \\t\\t} else {\\n162: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t}\\n165: \\n166: \\t// Build data structures for desired state.\\n167: \\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\\n168: \\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n169: \\n170: \\tfor _, pod := range pods {\\n171: \\t\\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\\n172: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n173: \\t\\t}\\n174: \\n175: \\t\\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\\n176: \\t\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\\n177: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n178: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n179: \\t\\t}\\n180: \\n181: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n182: \\t\\t\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\taddressType: addressType,\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\tports:       endpointPorts,\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\t\\t}\\n187: \\n188: \\t\\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\\n189: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n190: \\t\\t\\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\\n191: \\t\\t\\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\\n192: \\t\\t\\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\\n193: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n194: \\t\\t\\t}\\n195: \\t\\t\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\n196: \\t\\t\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\n197: \\t\\t\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\n198: \\t\\t\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\\n202: \\t\\t\\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"node\\\", klog.KRef(\\\"\\\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\\\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n206: \\t\\t\\t}\\n207: \\t\\t}\\n208: \\t\\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\\n209: \\t\\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\\n210: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\\n211: \\t\\t}\\n212: \\t}\\n213: \\n214: \\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\\n215: \\ttotalAdded := 0\\n216: \\ttotalRemoved := 0\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\\n219: \\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\\n220: \\t\\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\\n221: \\t\\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\\n222: \\t\\t\\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\\n223: \\n224: \\t\\ttotalAdded += added\\n225: \\t\\ttotalRemoved += removed\\n226: \\n227: \\t\\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\\n228: \\t\\t\\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\\n229: \\t\\t\\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\\n230: \\t\\t})\\n231: \\n232: \\t\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\\n233: \\t\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\\n234: \\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\\n238: \\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\\n239: \\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\\n240: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\\n241: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\\n242: \\t\\t}\\n243: \\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\\n246: \\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\\n247: \\t\\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\\n248: \\t\\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\\n249: \\t\\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\\n250: \\t\\t\\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\\n251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL1-250\"}",

    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package endpointslice\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"sort\"\n23: \t\"time\"\n24: \n25: \tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n26: \tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n27: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n28: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n31: \tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n33: \tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n34: \tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n37: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n38: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n39: \tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n40: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n41: )\n42: \n43: // Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n44: // desired state\n45: type Reconciler struct {\n46: \tclient               clientset.Interface\n47: \tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n48: \tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n49: \tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n50: \tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n51: \t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n52: \t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n53: \ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n54: \t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\n55: \t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\n56: \ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\n57: \t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\n58: \teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n59: \tcontrollerName string\n60: }\n61: \n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\n63: \n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\n67: \treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\n68: \t\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\n69: \t}\n70: }\n71: \n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\n73: // that logic in Reconciler\n74: type endpointMeta struct {\n75: \tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\n76: \taddressType discovery.AddressType\n77: }\n78: \n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n84: \tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n85: \terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n86: \tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n87: \n88: \t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\n89: \tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\n90: \n91: \t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\n92: \t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\n93: \t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\n94: \t// for further adjustment\n95: \tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n96: \t\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\n97: \t\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\n98: \t\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n99: \t\t\t\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\n100: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n101: \t\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\", \"existingSlice\", existingSlice, \"err\", err)\n102: \t\t\t\t} else {\n103: \t\t\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\n104: \t\t\t\t}\n105: \t\t\t}\n106: \n107: \t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n108: \t\t\tcontinue\n109: \t\t}\n110: \n111: \t\t// add list if it is not on our map\n112: \t\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\n113: \t\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\n114: \t\t}\n115: \n116: \t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\n117: \t}\n118: \n119: \t// reconcile for existing.\n120: \tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\n121: \t\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\n122: \t\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\n123: \t\tif err != nil {\n124: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n125: \t\t}\n126: \t}\n127: \n128: \t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\n129: \t// by the service\n130: \tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\n131: \t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n132: \t\tif err != nil {\n133: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\n134: \t\t} else {\n135: \t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\n136: \t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n137: \t\t}\n138: \t}\n139: \n140: \treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n141: }\n142: \n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\n148: \terrs := []error{}\n149: \n150: \tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n151: \tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n152: \tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n153: \tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\n154: \n155: \t// Build data structures for existing state.\n156: \texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n157: \tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n158: \t\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\n159: \t\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\n160: \t\t\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\n161: \t\t} else {\n162: \t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t}\n165: \n166: \t// Build data structures for desired state.\n167: \tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\n168: \tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n169: \n170: \tfor _, pod := range pods {\n171: \t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\n172: \t\t\tcontinue\n173: \t\t}\n174: \n175: \t\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\n176: \t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\n177: \t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n178: \t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n179: \t\t}\n180: \n181: \t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n182: \t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n183: \t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n184: \t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n185: \t\t\t}\n186: \t\t}\n187: \n188: \t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n189: \t\tif err != nil {\n190: \t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n191: \t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n192: \t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n193: \t\t\t\treturn err\n194: \t\t\t}\n195: \t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n196: \t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n197: \t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n198: \t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n199: \t\t\t// stuck forever.\n200: \t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n201: \t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n202: \t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n203: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n204: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n205: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n206: \t\t\t}\n207: \t\t}\n208: \t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n209: \t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n210: \t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n211: \t\t}\n212: \t}\n213: \n214: \tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\n215: \ttotalAdded := 0\n216: \ttotalRemoved := 0\n217: \n218: \t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\n219: \tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\n220: \t\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\n221: \t\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\n222: \t\t\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\n223: \n224: \t\ttotalAdded += added\n225: \t\ttotalRemoved += removed\n226: \n227: \t\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n228: \t\t\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\n229: \t\t\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\n230: \t\t})\n231: \n232: \t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\n233: \t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\n234: \t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\n235: \t}\n236: \n237: \t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\n238: \t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\n239: \tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\n240: \t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\n241: \t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\n242: \t\t}\n243: \t}\n244: \n245: \t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\n246: \tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\n247: \t\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\n248: \t\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\n249: \t\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\n250: \t\t\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\n251: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL1-250"
  }
}

\\t\\t* The value is optional. If given, it must begin with a letter or number, and may contain letters, numbers, hyphens, dots, and underscores, up to %[2]d characters.\\n78: \\t\\t* The effect must be NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule or NoExecute.\\n79: \\t\\t* Currently taint can only apply to node.`))\\n80: \\n81: \\ttaintExample = templates.Examples(i18n.T(`\\n82: \\t\\t# Update node 'foo' with a taint with key 'dedicated' and value 'special-user' and effect 'NoSchedule'\\n83: \\t\\t# If a taint with that key and effect already exists, its value is replaced as specified\\n84: \\t\\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated=special-user:NoSchedule\\n85: \\n86: \\t\\t# Remove from node 'foo' the taint with key 'dedicated' and effect 'NoSchedule' if one exists\\n87: \\t\\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated:NoSchedule-\\n88: \\n89: \\t\\t# Remove from node 'foo' all the taints with key 'dedicated'\\n90: \\t\\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated-\\n91: \\n92: \\t\\t# Add a taint with key 'dedicated' on nodes having label myLabel=X\\n93: \\t\\tkubectl taint node -l myLabel=X  dedicated=foo:PreferNoSchedule\\n94: \\n95: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":98,\"startLine\":77},{\"content\":\"249: \\t\\t\\t}\\n250: \\t\\t\\tif len(taintRemove.Effect) == 0 || taintAdd.Effect == taintRemove.Effect {\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\tconflictTaint := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s=%s\\\", taintRemove.Key, taintRemove.Effect)\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\tconflictTaints = append(conflictTaints, conflictTaint)\\n253: \\t\\t\\t}\\n254: \",\"endLine\":254,\"startLine\":249},{\"content\":\"107: \\tcmd := &cobra.Command{\\n108: \\t\\tUse:                   \\\"taint NODE NAME KEY_1=VAL_1:TAINT_EFFECT_1 ... KEY_N=VAL_N:TAINT_EFFECT_N\\\",\\n109: \\t\\tDisableFlagsInUseLine: true,\\n110: \\t\\tShort:                 i18n.T(\\\"Update the taints on one or more nodes\\\"),\\n111: \\t\\tLong:                  fmt.Sprintf(taintLong, validation.DNS1123SubdomainMaxLength, validation.LabelValueMaxLength),\\n112: \\t\\tExample:               taintExample,\\n113: \\t\\tValidArgsFunction:     completion.SpecifiedResourceTypeAndNameCompletionFunc(f, validArgs),\\n114: \",\"endLine\":114,\"startLine\":107}],\"file\":\"staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/taint.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/taint.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"418: \\n419: func toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(taints []v1.Taint, tolerations []v1.Toleration) bool {\\n420: \\tfilteredTaints := []v1.Taint{}\\n421: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n422: \\t\\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule {\\n423: \\t\\t\\tfilteredTaints = append(filteredTaints, taint)\\n424: \\t\\t}\\n425: \",\"endLine\":425,\"startLine\":418},{\"content\":\"415: \\n416: \\treturn toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(n.Spec.Taints, nil)\\n417: }\\n418: \",\"endLine\":418,\"startLine\":415},{\"content\":\"59: var (\\n60: \\t// unreachableTaintTemplate is the taint for when a node becomes unreachable.\\n61: \\t// Copied from pkg/controller/nodelifecycle to avoid pulling extra dependencies\\n62: \\tunreachableTaintTemplate = &v1.Taint{\\n63: \\t\\tKey:    v1.TaintNodeUnreachable,\\n64: \\t\\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\\n65: \\t}\\n66: '127.0.0.1:'\\n86: \\n87:   create_test_pods\\n88: \\n89:   # taint/untaint\\n90:   # Pre-condition: node doesn't have dedicated=foo:PreferNoSchedule taint\\n91:   kube::test::get_object_assert \\n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n24: \\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n25: \\t\\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\\n26: \\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\\n27: \\t}\\n28:           {
            "content": "77: \t\t* The value is optional. If given, it must begin with a letter or number, and may contain letters, numbers, hyphens, dots, and underscores, up to %[2]d characters.\n78: \t\t* The effect must be NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule or NoExecute.\n79: \t\t* Currently taint can only apply to node.`))\n80: \n81: \ttaintExample = templates.Examples(i18n.T(`\n82: \t\t# Update node 'foo' with a taint with key 'dedicated' and value 'special-user' and effect 'NoSchedule'\n83: \t\t# If a taint with that key and effect already exists, its value is replaced as specified\n84: \t\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated=special-user:NoSchedule\n85: \n86: \t\t# Remove from node 'foo' the taint with key 'dedicated' and effect 'NoSchedule' if one exists\n87: \t\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated:NoSchedule-\n88: \n89: \t\t# Remove from node 'foo' all the taints with key 'dedicated'\n90: \t\tkubectl taint nodes foo dedicated-\n91: \n92: \t\t# Add a taint with key 'dedicated' on nodes having label myLabel=X\n93: \t\tkubectl taint node -l myLabel=X  dedicated=foo:PreferNoSchedule\n94: \n95: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 98,
            "startLine": 77
          },
          {
            "content": "249: \t\t\t}\n250: \t\t\tif len(taintRemove.Effect) == 0 || taintAdd.Effect == taintRemove.Effect {\n251: \t\t\t\tconflictTaint := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s=%s\", taintRemove.Key, taintRemove.Effect)\n252: \t\t\t\tconflictTaints = append(conflictTaints, conflictTaint)\n253: \t\t\t}\n254: ",
            "endLine": 254,
            "startLine": 249
          },
          {
            "content": "107: \tcmd := &cobra.Command{\n108: \t\tUse:                   \"taint NODE NAME KEY_1=VAL_1:TAINT_EFFECT_1 ... KEY_N=VAL_N:TAINT_EFFECT_N\",\n109: \t\tDisableFlagsInUseLine: true,\n110: \t\tShort:                 i18n.T(\"Update the taints on one or more nodes\"),\n111: \t\tLong:                  fmt.Sprintf(taintLong, validation.DNS1123SubdomainMaxLength, validation.LabelValueMaxLength),\n112: \t\tExample:               taintExample,\n113: \t\tValidArgsFunction:     completion.SpecifiedResourceTypeAndNameCompletionFunc(f, validArgs),\n114: ",
            "endLine": 114,
            "startLine": 107
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/taint.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/taint.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "418: \n419: func toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(taints []v1.Taint, tolerations []v1.Toleration) bool {\n420: \tfilteredTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n421: \tfor _, taint := range taints {\n422: \t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule {\n423: \t\t\tfilteredTaints = append(filteredTaints, taint)\n424: \t\t}\n425: ",
            "endLine": 425,
            "startLine": 418
          },
          {
            "content": "415: \n416: \treturn toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(n.Spec.Taints, nil)\n417: }\n418: ",
            "endLine": 418,
            "startLine": 415
          },
          {
            "content": "59: var (\n60: \t// unreachableTaintTemplate is the taint for when a node becomes unreachable.\n61: \t// Copied from pkg/controller/nodelifecycle to avoid pulling extra dependencies\n62: \tunreachableTaintTemplate = &v1.Taint{\n63: \t\tKey:    v1.TaintNodeUnreachable,\n64: \t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n65: \t}\n66: ",
            "endLine": 66,
            "startLine": 59
          }
        ],

          {

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "20: \n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n24: \treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n25: \t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\n26: \t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\n27: \t}\n28: ",
            "endLine": 28,
            "startLine": 20
          }
        ],

Input
{
  "endLine": 3540,
