Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3500: \\t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\\n3501: \\t// It is only written for NoExecute taints.\\n3502: \\t// +optional\\n3503: \\tTimeAdded *metav1.Time `json:\\\"timeAdded,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=timeAdded\\\"`\\n3504: }\\n3505: \\n3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: const (\\n3510: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3511: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3512: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3513: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3514: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3515: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3516: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3517: \\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3518: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3519: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3520: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3521: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \\n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\\n3532: type Toleration struct {\\n3533: \\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\\n3534: \\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\\n3535: \\t// +optional\\n3536: \\tKey string `json:\\\"key,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n3537: \\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\\n3538: \\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\\n3539: \\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\\n3540: \\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\\n3541: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3500-3540\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3500: \t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\n3501: \t// It is only written for NoExecute taints.\n3502: \t// +optional\n3503: \tTimeAdded *metav1.Time `json:\"timeAdded,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=timeAdded\"`\n3504: }\n3505: \n3506: // +enum\n3507: type TaintEffect string\n3508: \n3509: const (\n3510: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3511: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3512: \t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n3513: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3514: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3515: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3516: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3517: \t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\n3518: \tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n3519: \t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n3520: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n3521: \t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: \n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n3532: type Toleration struct {\n3533: \t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n3534: \t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n3535: \t// +optional\n3536: \tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n3537: \t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n3538: \t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n3539: \t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\n3540: \t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n3541: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3500-3540"
  }
}
27. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "--- a/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n+++ b/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n@@ -3524,6 +3524,11 @@ const (\n\n \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n+\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\n+\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\n+\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n+\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\n+\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n )\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging_k8s.io_api_core_v1_types.go.patch"
}
Output
{
  "content": "--- a/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n+++ b/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n@@ -3524,6 +3524,11 @@ const (\n\n \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n+\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\n+\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\n+\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n+\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\n+\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n )\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/staging_k8s.io_api_core_v1_types.go.patch",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

30. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "--- Replace the TaintEffect constants section with this:\nThe section at line 3509-3528 should become:\n\nconst (\n\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\n\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n\n\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\n\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\n\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\n\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n)\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go.patch"
}

argument to specify on a context when you want to list or filter resources across all namespaces\\n30: \\tNamespaceAll string = \\\"\\\"\\n31: \\t// NamespaceNodeLease is the namespace where we place container in the pod.\\n36: type Volume struct {\\n37: \\t// name of the volume.\\n38: \\t// Must be a DNS_LABEL and unique within the pod.\\n39: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names\\n40: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n41: \\t// volumeSource represents the location and type of the mounted volume.\\n42: \\t// If not specified, the Volume is implied to be an EmptyDir.\\n43: \\t// This implied behavior is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.\\n44: \\tVolumeSource `json:\\\",inline\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=volumeSource\\\"`\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: // Represents the source of a volume to mount.\\n48: // Only one of its members may be specified.\\n49: type VolumeSource struct {\\n50: \\t// hostPath represents a pre-existing file or directory on the host\\n51: \\t// machine that is directly exposed to the container. This is generally\\n52: \\t// used for system agents or other privileged things that are allowed\\n53: \\t// to see the host machine. Most containers will NOT need this.\\n54: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\n55: \\t// ---\\n56: \\t// TODO(jonesdl) We need to restrict who can use host directory mounts and who can/can not\\n57: \\t// mount host directories as read/write.\\n58: \\t// +optional\\n59: \\tHostPath *HostPathVolumeSource `json:\\\"hostPath,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=hostPath\\\"`\\n60: \\t// emptyDir represents a temporary directory that shares a pod's lifetime.\\n61: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\n62: \\t// +optional\\n63: \\tEmptyDir *EmptyDirVolumeSource `json:\\\"emptyDir,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=emptyDir\\\"`\\n64: \\t// gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a\\n65: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n66: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk\\n67: \\t// +optional\\n68: \\tGCEPersistentDisk *GCEPersistentDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\"gcePersistentDisk,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=gcePersistentDisk\\\"`\\n69: \\t// awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a\\n70: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n71: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore\\n72: \\t// +optional\\n73: \\tAWSElasticBlockStore *AWSElasticBlockStoreVolumeSource `json:\\\"awsElasticBlockStore,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=awsElasticBlockStore\\\"`\\n74: \\t// gitRepo represents a git repository at a particular revision.\\n75: \\t// DEPRECATED: GitRepo is deprecated. host\n51: \t// machine that is directly exposed to the container. 32. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/files_to_modify.txt << 'EOF'\n1. staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go - Add TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic constant\n2. pkg/apis/core/types.go - Add TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic constant\n3. pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go - Update validateTaintEffect\n4. staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go - Update validateTaintEffect\n5. pkg/util/taints/taints.go - Update validateTaintEffect\n6. pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go - Update DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc\n7. staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go - Update podToEndpoint logic or add node taint checks\n8. staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go - Add node taint checking for endpoints\nEOF\ncat /tmp/files_to_modify.txt\n"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,

      "text": "Now let me create a comprehensive final summary:\n\n## Summary: TaintEffect Constants and Usage in kubernetes v1.30.0\n\nBased on my search through the repository, here are all the locations where TaintEffect constants are defined and used:\n\n### 1. TaintEffect Constant Definitions\n\n**File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go`**\n- **Lines 3506-3529**: TaintEffect type definition and constants\n  - Line 3507: `type TaintEffect string`\n  - Line 3514: `TaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"`\n  - Line 3518: `TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"`\n  - Line 3527: `TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"`\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/apis/core/types.go`**\n- **Lines 3162-3187**: Identical TaintEffect type definition and constants\n  - Line 3164: `type TaintEffect string`\n  - Line 3172: `TaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"`\n  - Line 3176: `TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"`\n  - Line 3185: `TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"`\n\n### 2. Taint Effect Validation Functions\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go`**\n- **Lines 3858-3879**: Main validation function for taint effects\n  - Line 3858: `func validateTaintEffect(effect *core.TaintEffect, allowEmpty bool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList`\n  - Lines 3864-3866: Switch statement validating against `TaintEffectNoSchedule`, `TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule`, `TaintEffectNoExecute`\n  - Lines 3869-3872: Array of valid values for error reporting\n  \n- **Lines 6022-6045**: Node taints validation\n  - Line 6023: `func validateNodeTaints(taints []core.Taint, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList`\n  - Line 6037: Calls `validateTaintEffect()` for each taint\n  \n- **Lines 3954-3957**: Toleration validation for NoExecute effect\n  - Line 3954: Checks if `toleration.Effect != core.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n  \n- **Lines 3975-3978**: Toleration effect validation\n  - Line 3977: Calls `validateTaintEffect()` for toleration effects\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/util/taints/taints.go`**\n- **Lines 81-87**: Utility function for taint effect validation\n  - Line 81: `func validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error`\n  - Line 82: Checks against all three effects: `TaintEffectNoSchedule`, `TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule`, `TaintEffectNoExecute`\n\n**File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go`**\n- **Lines 120-123**: kubectl taint command validation\n  - Line 120: `func validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error`\n  - Line 121: Checks against all three effects\n\n### 3. Taint Filtering for Scheduling\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go`**\n- **Lines 21-28**: Filter function for Do-Not-Schedule taints\n  - Line 23: `func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool`\n  - Line 26: Filters for `t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/tainttoleration/taint_toleration.go`**\n- **Lines 64-74**: Pod toleration filter plugin\n  - Line 64: `func (pl *TaintToleration) Filter(...) *framework.Status`\n  - Line 67: Uses `helper.DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc()` to filter taints\n  - Line 67: Uses `v1helper.FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint()` for taint checking\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/scheduler/eventhandlers.go`**\n- **Lines 613-616**: Event handler for pod scheduling with NoSchedule taint\n  - Line 613: Calls `FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint()` \n  - Line 614: Filter returns `t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule`\n\n### 4. Kubelet Taint Checking\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle/predicate.go`**\n- **Lines 299-302**: Kubelet pod admission checks\n  - Line 299: `FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint()` \n  - Line 301: Filter checks `t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n  - Line 300-301: Comment states \"Kubelet is only interested in the NoExecute taint\"\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/util/tolerations/tolerations.go`**\n- **Line 89**: Toleration seconds validation for NoExecute\n  - Line 89: `if ss.Effect == api.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n\n### 5. Daemon Controller Taint Checking\n\n**File: `/workspace/pkg/controller/daemon/daemon_controller.go`**\n- **Lines 1294-1296**: Daemon pod taint tolerance check\n  - Line 1294: `FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint()`\n  - Line 1295: Filter checks `t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n  \n- **Lines 1308-1310**: Daemon predicates function\n  - Line 1308: `FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint()`\n  - Line 1309: Filter checks `t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule`\n\n### 6. EndpointSlice (Note: No direct taint handling found)\n\n**File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go`**\n- No direct taint effect checks were found in the endpointslice reconciler\n\n**File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go`**\n- No direct taint effect checks were found in the endpointslice utils\n\n## Key Findings:\n\n1. **TaintEffect constants are mirrored** in both `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go` and `pkg/apis/core/types.go` (internal API types)\n\n2. **Three valid taint effects** are consistently validated:\n   - `TaintEffectNoSchedule` - Prevents new pod scheduling (enforced by scheduler)\n   - `TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule` - Soft constraint for scheduling\n   - `TaintEffectNoExecute` - Evicts running pods (enforced by NodeController and Kubelet)\n\n3. **Validation occurs at multiple layers**:\n   - API validation level (`pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go`)\n   - Utility level (`pkg/util/taints/taints.go`)\n   - CLI level (`staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go`)\n\n4. **Scheduling filter uses selective taint checking**:\n   - Scheduler framework filters both `NoSchedule` and `NoExecute` taints\n   - Kubelet only cares about `NoExecute` taints\n   - Daemon controller checks both for scheduling decisions\n\n5. **TolerationSeconds validation** (line 3954 in validation.go) is only allowed for `TaintEffectNoExecute` effect",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

untolerated taint\\n77: // Returns false if all taints are tolerated\\n78: func FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint(taints []v1.Taint, tolerations []v1.Toleration, inclusionFilter taintsFilterFunc) may run PostFilter (if enabled) in both cases.\\n414: \\tpostFilterMsg := f.Diagnosis.PostFilterMsg\\n415: \\tif postFilterMsg != \\\"\\\" {\\n416: \\t\\treasonMsg += fmt.Sprintf(\\\" %v\\\", \",\"endLine\":115,\"startLine\":110},{\"content\":\"382: \\n383: \\tif preFilterMsg == \\\"\\\" {\\n384: \\t\\t// the scheduling cycle went through PreFilter extension point successfully.\\n385: The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\\n3151: \\t// +optional\\n3152: \",\"endLine\":3152,\"startLine\":3143},{\"content\":\"3162: \\n3163: // TaintEffect defines the effects of Taint\\n3164: type TaintEffect string\\n3165: \\n3166: // These are valid values for TaintEffect\\n3167: const (\\n3168: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3169: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3170: \",\"endLine\":3170,\"startLine\":3162}],\"file\":\"pkg/apis/core/types.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/types.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1804: #### Network\\n1805: - EndpointSlices: If upgrading a cluster with EndpointSlices already enabled, any EndpointSlices that should be managed by the EndpointSlice controller should have a `endpointslice.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label set to `endpointslice-controller.k8s.io`.\\n1806: \\n1807: \",\"endLine\":1807,\"startLine\":1804},{\"content\":\"2621: * kube-controller-manager: Fixes bug setting headless service labels on endpoints ([#85361](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/85361), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))\\n2622: * When upgrading to 1.17 with a cluster with EndpointSlices enabled, the `endpointslice.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label needs to be set on each EndpointSlice. ([#85359](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/85359), [@robscott](https://github.com/ro ... [line truncated]\\n2623: * Remove redundant API validation when using Service Topology with externalTrafficPolicy=Local ([#85346](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/85346), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim))\\n2624: \",\"endLine\":2624,\"startLine\":2621},{\"content\":\"2120: - Significant kube-proxy performance improvements for non UDP ports. ([#83208](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83208), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2121: - Improved performance of kube-proxy with EndpointSlice enabled with more efficient sorting. ([#83035](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/83035), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2122: - EndpointSlices are now beta for better Network Endpoint performance at scale. ([#84390](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84390), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2123: - Updated EndpointSlices to use PublishNotReadyAddresses from Services. ([#84573](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/84573), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott))\\n2124: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2131,\"startLine\":2120}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.17.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"168: \\n169: // TestTaintEvictionControllerGating ensures that it is possible to run taint-manager as a separated controller\\n170: // only when the SeparateTaintEvictionController feature is enabled\\n171: func TestTaintEvictionControllerGating(t *testing.T) {\\n172: \\ttests := []struct {\\n173: \",\"endLine\":173,\"startLine\":168},{\"content\":\"200: \\n201: \\t\\t\\ttaintEvictionControllerDescriptor := NewControllerDescriptors()[names.TaintEvictionController]\\n202: \\t\\t\\ttaintEvictionControllerDescriptor.initFunc = func(ctx context.Context, controllerContext ControllerContext, controllerName string) (controller controllermanagercontroller.Interface, enabled bool, err error) {\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tinitFuncCalled = true\\n204: \",\"endLine\":204,\"startLine\":200},{\"content\":\"54: \\t\\tnames.EndpointsController,\\n55: \\t\\tnames.EndpointSliceController,\\n56: \\t\\tnames.EndpointSliceMirroringController,\\n57: \\t\\tnames.ReplicationControllerController,\\n58: \",\"endLine\":58,\"startLine\":54}],\"file\":\"cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager_test.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager_test.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2676: - Document that ServiceTopology feature is required to use `service.spec.topologyKeys`. ([#96528](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96528), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim)) [SIG Apps]\\n2677: - EndpointSlice has a new NodeName field guarded by the EndpointSliceNodeName feature gate.\\n2678:   - EndpointSlice topology field will be deprecated in an upcoming release.\\n2679:   - EndpointSlice \\\"IP\\\" address type is formally removed after being deprecated in Kubernetes 1.17.\\n2680:   - The discovery.k8s.io/v1alpha1 API is deprecated and will be removed in Kubernetes 1.21. ([#96440](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96440), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\\n2681: \",\"endLine\":2681,\"startLine\":2676},{\"content\":\"1998: - Document that ServiceTopology feature is required to use `service.spec.topologyKeys`. ([#96528](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96528), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim)) [SIG Apps]\\n1999: - EndpointSlice has a new NodeName field guarded by the EndpointSliceNodeName feature gate.\\n2000:   - EndpointSlice topology field will be deprecated in an upcoming release.\\n2001:   - EndpointSlice \\\"IP\\\" address type is formally removed after being deprecated in Kubernetes 1.17.\\n2002:   - The discovery.k8s.io/v1alpha1 API is deprecated and will be removed in Kubernetes 1.21. ([#96440](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96440), [@robscott](https://github.com/robscott)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\\n2003: \",\"endLine\":2003,\"startLine\":1998},{\"content\":\"598: \\n599: - EndpointSlice Mirroring controller now cleans up managed EndpointSlices when a Service selector is added (#106136, @robscott) [SIG Apps, Network and Testing]\\n600: - Fix concurrent map access causing panics when logging timed-out API calls. (#106124, @marseel) [SIG API Machinery]\\n601: \",\"endLine\":601,\"startLine\":598}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"6021: \\n6022: // validateNodeTaints tests if given taints have valid data.\\n6023: func validateNodeTaints(taints []core.Taint, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\\n6024: \\tallErrors := field.ErrorList{}\\n6025: \",\"endLine\":6025,\"startLine\":6021},{\"content\":\"3857: \\n3858: func validateTaintEffect(effect *core.TaintEffect, allowEmpty bool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\\n3859: \\tif !allowEmpty && len(*effect) == 0 {\\n3860: \",\"endLine\":3860,\"startLine\":3857},{\"content\":\"6025: \\n6026: \\tuniqueTaints := map[core.TaintEffect]sets.Set[string]{}\\n6027: \\n6028: \\tfor i, currTaint := range taints {\\n6029: \\t\\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\\n6030: \\t\\t// validate the taint key\\n6031: \\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, unversionedvalidation.ValidateLabelName(currTaint.Key, idxPath.Child(\\\"key\\\"))...)\\n6032: \\t\\t// validate the taint value\\n6033: \\t\\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(currTaint.Value); len(errs) != 0 {\\n6034: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"value\\\"), currTaint.Value, strings.Join(errs, \\\";\\\")))\\n6035: \\t\\t}\\n6036: \\t\\t// validate the taint effect\\n6037: \\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, validateTaintEffect(&currTaint.Effect, false, idxPath.Child(\\\"effect\\\"))...)\\n6038: \\n6039: \\t\\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\\n6040: \\t\\tif len(uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect].Has(currTaint.Key) {\\n6041: \\t\\t\\tduplicatedError := field.Duplicate(idxPath, currTaint)\\n6042: \\t\\t\\tduplicatedError.Detail = \\\"taints must be unique by key and effect pair\\\"\\n6043: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":6044,\"startLine\":6025}],\"file\":\"pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2361:   - PreemptionByKubeScheduler (Pod preempted by kube-scheduler)\\n2362:   - DeletionByTaintManager (Pod deleted by taint manager due to NoExecute taint)\\n2363:   - EvictionByEvictionAPI (Pod evicted by Eviction API)\\n2364: \",\"endLine\":2364,\"startLine\":2361},{\"content\":\"2993:   - PreemptionByKubeScheduler (Pod preempted by kube-scheduler)\\n2994:   - DeletionByTaintManager (Pod deleted by taint manager due to NoExecute taint)\\n2995:   - EvictionByEvictionAPI (Pod evicted by Eviction API)\\n2996: \",\"endLine\":2996,\"startLine\":2993},{\"content\":\"2529: - Kubeadm: now sets the host `OS` environment variables when executing `crictl` during image pulls. This fixed a bug where `*PROXY` environment variables did not affect `crictl` internet connectivity. ([#110134](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pul ... [line truncated]\\n2530: - Kubeadm: only taint control plane nodes when the legacy \\\"master\\\" taint is present. This avoids the bug where \\\"kubeadm upgrade\\\" will re-taint a control plane  node with the new \\\"control plane\\\" taint even if the user explicitly untainted the node. ([#10984 ... [line truncated]\\n2531: - Kubeadm: respect user specified image repository when using Kubernetes ci version ([#111017](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/111017), [@SataQiu](https://github.com/SataQiu)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]\\n2532: \",\"endLine\":2532,\"startLine\":2529}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3487: \\n3488: // The node this Taint is attached to has the \\\"effect\\\" on\\n3489: // any pod that does not tolerate the Taint.\\n3490: type Taint struct {\\n3491: \\t// Required. The taint key to be applied to a node.\\n3492: \\tKey string `json:\\\"key\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n3493: \\t// The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\\n3494: \\t// +optional\\n3495: \",\"endLine\":3495,\"startLine\":3487},{\"content\":\"3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: \",\"endLine\":3509,\"startLine\":3506},{\"content\":\"3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \",\"endLine\":3529,\"startLine\":3522}],\"file\":\"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3191: * Introduce Alpha field `Immutable` in both Secret and ConfigMap objects to mark their contents as immutable. The implementation is hidden behind feature gate `ImmutableEphemeralVolumes` (currently in Alpha stage). ([#86377](https://github.com/kubernetes/k ... [line truncated]\\n3192: * EndpointSlices will now be enabled by default. A new `EndpointSliceProxying` feature gate determines if kube-proxy will use EndpointSlices, this is disabled by default. ([#86137](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/86137), [@robscott](https://g ... [line truncated]\\n3193: * kubeadm upgrades always persist the etcd backup for stacked ([#86861](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/86861), [@SataQiu](https://github.com/SataQiu))\\n3194: \",\"endLine\":3194,\"startLine\":3191},{\"content\":\"2355: - Elasticsearch supports automatically setting the advertise address ([#85944](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/85944), [@SataQiu](https://github.com/SataQiu)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]\\n2356: - EndpointSlices will now be enabled by default. A new `EndpointSliceProxying` feature gate determines if kube-proxy will use EndpointSlices, this is disabled by default. ([#86137](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/86137), [@robscott](https://g ... [line truncated]\\n2357: - Kube-proxy: Added dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 support to the iptables proxier. ([#82462](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/82462), [@vllry](https://github.com/vllry)) [SIG Network]\\n2358: \",\"endLine\":2358,\"startLine\":2355},{\"content\":\"1326: - Fixed bug in reflector that couldn't recover from \\\"Too large resource version\\\" errors with API servers 1.17.0-1.18.5 ([#94316](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94316), [@janeczku](https://github.com/janeczku)) [SIG API Machinery]\\n1327: - Fixed the EndpointSliceController to correctly create endpoints for IPv6-only pods.\\n1328:   \\n1329: \",\"endLine\":1329,\"startLine\":1326}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.18.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.18.md\"}]}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: const (\\n3510: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3511: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3512: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3513: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3514: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3515: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3516: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3517: \\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3518: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3519: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3520: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3521: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \\n3530: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3506-3529\"}"

}
Output
"{\"content\":\"3162: \\n3163: // TaintEffect defines the effects of Taint\\n3164: type TaintEffect string\\n3165: \\n3166: // These are valid values for TaintEffect\\n3167: const (\\n3168: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3169: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3170: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3171: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3172: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3173: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3174: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3175: \\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3176: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3177: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3178: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3179: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3180: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3181: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3182: \\n3183: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3184: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3185: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3186: )\\n3187: \\n3188: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/types.go%3FL3162-3187\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"3857: \\n3858: func validateTaintEffect(effect *core.TaintEffect, allowEmpty bool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\\n3859: \\tif !allowEmpty && len(*effect) == 0 {\\n3860: \\t\\treturn field.ErrorList{field.Required(fldPath, \\\"\\\")}\\n3861: \\t}\\n3862: \\n3863: \\tallErrors := field.ErrorList{}\\n3864: \\tswitch *effect {\\n3865: \\t// TODO: Replace next line with subsequent commented-out line when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\\n3866: \\tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\\n3867: \\t\\t// case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\\n3868: \\tdefault:\\n3869: \\t\\tvalidValues := []core.TaintEffect{\\n3870: \\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\\n3871: \\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\\n3872: \\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,\\n3873: \\t\\t\\t// TODO: Uncomment this block when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\\n3874: \\t\\t\\t// core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit,\\n3875: \\t\\t}\\n3876: \\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *effect, validValues))\\n3877: \\t}\\n3878: \\treturn allErrors\\n3879: }\\n3880: \\n3881: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go%3FL3857-3880\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package helper\\n18: \\n19: import v1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n20: \\n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n24: \\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n25: \\t\\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\\n26: \\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\\n27: \\t}\\n28: }\\n29: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go\"}"

"{\"content\":\"6021: \\n6022: // validateNodeTaints tests if given taints have valid data.\\n6023: func validateNodeTaints(taints []core.Taint, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\\n6024: \\tallErrors := field.ErrorList{}\\n6025: \\n6026: \\tuniqueTaints := map[core.TaintEffect]sets.Set[string]{}\\n6027: \\n6028: \\tfor i, currTaint := range taints {\\n6029: \\t\\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\\n6030: \\t\\t// validate the taint key\\n6031: \\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, unversionedvalidation.ValidateLabelName(currTaint.Key, idxPath.Child(\\\"key\\\"))...)\\n6032: \\t\\t// validate the taint value\\n6033: \\t\\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(currTaint.Value); len(errs) != 0 {\\n6034: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"value\\\"), currTaint.Value, strings.Join(errs, \\\";\\\")))\\n6035: \\t\\t}\\n6036: \\t\\t// validate the taint effect\\n6037: \\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, validateTaintEffect(&currTaint.Effect, false, idxPath.Child(\\\"effect\\\"))...)\\n6038: \\n6039: \\t\\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\\n6040: \\t\\tif len(uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[currTaint.Effect].Has(currTaint.Key) {\\n6041: \\t\\t\\tduplicatedError := field.Duplicate(idxPath, currTaint)\\n6042: \\t\\t\\tduplicatedError.Detail = \\\"taints must be unique by key and effect pair\\\"\\n6043: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, duplicatedError)\\n6044: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n6045: \\t\\t}\\n6046: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go%3FL6021-6045\"}"

"{\"content\":\"60: \\t}\\n61: }\\n62: \\n63: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\\n64: func (pl *TaintToleration) Filter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\\n65: \\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\\n66: \\n67: \\ttaint, isUntolerated := v1helper.FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint(node.Spec.Taints, pod.Spec.Tolerations, helper.DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc())\\n68: \\tif !isUntolerated {\\n69: \\t\\treturn nil\\n70: \\t}\\n71: \\n72: \\terrReason := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"node(s) had untolerated taint {%s: %s}\\\", taint.Key, taint.Value)\\n73: \\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, errReason)\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: // preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\\n77: type preScoreState struct {\\n78: \\ttolerationsPreferNoSchedule []v1.Toleration\\n79: }\\n80: \\n81: // Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. transforming current EndpointSlice state into\\n44: // desired state\\n45: type Reconciler struct {\\n46: \\tclient               clientset.Interface\\n47: \\tnodeLister tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\\n52: \\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\\n53: \\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\\n54: \\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\\n55: \\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\\n56: \\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\\n57: \\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\\n58: \\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\\n59: \\tcontrollerName string\\n60: }\\n61: \\n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\\n63: \\n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\\n67: \\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\\n68: \\t\\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\\n69: \\t}\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\\n73: // that logic in Reconciler\\n74: type endpointMeta struct {\\n75: \\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\\n76: \\taddressType discovery.AddressType\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\\n84: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\\n85: \\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\\n86: \\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\\n87: \\n88: \\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\\n89: \\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\\n90: \\n91: \\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\\n92: \\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\\n93: \\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\\n94: \\t// for further adjustment\\n95: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n96: \\t\\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\\n97: \\t\\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\\n98: \\t\\t\\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\\n100: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL1-100\"}"

specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package endpointslice\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n25: \\tapiequality \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\\\"\\n26: \\tapimachineryvalidation \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\\\"\\n27: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n31: \\tendpointutil \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n33: \\tutilnet \\\"k8s.io/utils/net\\\"\\n34: )\\n35: \\n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\\n38: \\tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\\n39: \\tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\\n40: \\t// For compatibility reasons, \\\"ready\\\" should never be \\\"true\\\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\\n41: \\t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\\n42: \\tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\\n43: \\tep := discovery.Endpoint{\\n44: \\t\\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\\n45: \\t\\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\\n46: \\t\\t\\tReady:       &ready,\\n47: \\t\\t\\tServing:     &serving,\\n48: \\t\\t\\tTerminating: &terminating,\\n49: \\t\\t},\\n50: \\t\\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\\n51: \\t\\t\\tKind:      \\\"Pod\\\",\\n52: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\\n53: \\t\\t\\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\\n54: \\t\\t\\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\\n55: \\t\\t},\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \\\"\\\" {\\n59: \\t\\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\\n60: \\t}\\n61: \\n62: \\tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \\\"\\\" {\\n63: \\t\\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\\n64: \\t\\tep.Zone = &zone\\n65: \\t}\\n66: \\n67: \\tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\\n68: \\t\\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\\n69: \\t}\\n70: \\n71: \\treturn ep\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // getEndpointPorts returns a list of EndpointPorts generated from a Service\\n75: // and Pod.\\n76: func getEndpointPorts(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, pod *v1.Pod) []discovery.EndpointPort {\\n77: \\tendpointPorts := []discovery.EndpointPort{}\\n78: \\n79: \\t// Allow headless service not to have ports.\\n80: \\tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\\n81: \\t\\treturn endpointPorts\\n82: \\t}\\n83: \\n84: \\tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\\n85: \\t\\tservicePort := &service.Spec.Ports[i]\\n86: \\n87: \\t\\tportName := servicePort.Name\\n88: \\t\\tportProto := servicePort.Protocol\\n89: \\t\\tportNum, err := findPort(pod, servicePort)\\n90: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n91: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Failed to find port for service\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"err\\\", err)\\n92: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n93: \\t\\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\t\\ti32PortNum := int32(portNum)\\n96: \\t\\tendpointPorts = append(endpointPorts, discovery.EndpointPort{\\n97: \\t\\t\\tName:        &portName,\\n98: \\t\\t\\tPort:        &i32PortNum,\\n99: \\t\\t\\tProtocol:    &portProto,\\n100: \\t\\t\\tAppProtocol: servicePort.AppProtocol,\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go%3FL1-100\"}"

"{\"content\":\"295: \\t}\\n296: \\n297: \\t// Check taint/toleration except for static pods\\n298: \\tif !types.IsStaticPod(pod) {\\n299: \\t\\t_, isUntolerated := corev1.FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint(nodeInfo.Node().Spec.Taints, pod.Spec.Tolerations, func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n300: \\t\\t\\t// Kubelet is only interested in the NoExecute taint.\\n301: \\t\\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\\n302: \\t\\t})\\n303: \\t\\tif isUntolerated {\\n304: \\t\\t\\treasons = append(reasons, &PredicateFailureError{tainttoleration.Name, tainttoleration.ErrReasonNotMatch})\\n305: \\t\\t}\\n306: \\t}\\n307: \\n308: \\treturn reasons\\n309: }\\n310: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle/predicate.go%3FL295-310\"}"

"{\"content\":\"3950: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"operator\\\"), toleration.Operator,\\n3951: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"operator must be Exists when `key` is empty, which means \\\\\\\"match all values and all keys\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n3952: \\t\\t}\\n3953: \\n3954: \\t\\tif toleration.TolerationSeconds != nil && toleration.Effect != core.TaintEffectNoExecute {\\n3955: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"effect\\\"), toleration.Effect,\\n3956: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"effect must be 'NoExecute' when `tolerationSeconds` is set\\\"))\\n3957: \\t\\t}\\n3958: \\n3959: \\t\\t// validate toleration operator and value\\n3960: \\t\\tswitch toleration.Operator {\\n3961: \\t\\t// empty operator means Equal\\n3962: \\t\\tcase core.TolerationOpEqual, \\\"\\\":\\n3963: \\t\\t\\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(toleration.Value); len(errs) != 0 {\\n3964: \\t\\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"operator\\\"), toleration.Value, strings.Join(errs, \\\";\\\")))\\n3965: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3966: \\t\\tcase core.TolerationOpExists:\\n3967: \\t\\t\\tif len(toleration.Value) > 0 {\\n3968: \\t\\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\\\"operator\\\"), toleration, \\\"value must be empty when `operator` is 'Exists'\\\"))\\n3969: \\t\\t\\t}\\n3970: \\t\\tdefault:\\n3971: \\t\\t\\tvalidValues := []core.TolerationOperator{core.TolerationOpEqual, core.TolerationOpExists}\\n3972: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.NotSupported(idxPath.Child(\\\"operator\\\"), toleration.Operator, validValues))\\n3973: \\t\\t}\\n3974: \\n3975: \\t\\t// validate toleration effect, empty toleration effect means match all taint effects\\n3976: \\t\\tif len(toleration.Effect) > 0 {\\n3977: \\t\\t\\tallErrors = append(allErrors, validateTaintEffect(&toleration.Effect, true, idxPath.Child(\\\"effect\\\"))...)\\n3978: \\t\\t}\\n3979: \\t}\\n3980: \\treturn allErrors\\n3981: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go%3FL3950-3980\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1290: \\t}\\n1291: \\n1292: \\tif !fitsTaints {\\n1293: \\t\\t// Scheduled daemon pods should continue running if they tolerate NoExecute taint.\\n1294: \\t\\t_, hasUntoleratedTaint := v1helper.FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint(taints, pod.Spec.Tolerations, func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n1295: \\t\\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\\n1296: \\t\\t})\\n1297: \\t\\treturn false, !hasUntoleratedTaint\\n1298: \\t}\\n1299: \\n1300: \\treturn true, true\\n1301: }\\n1302: \\n1303: // predicates checks if a DaemonSet's pod can run on a node.\\n1304: func predicates(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, taints []v1.Taint) (fitsNodeName, fitsNodeAffinity, fitsTaints bool) {\\n1305: \\tfitsNodeName = len(pod.Spec.NodeName) == 0 || pod.Spec.NodeName == node.Name\\n1306: \\t// Ignore parsing errors for backwards compatibility.\\n1307: \\tfitsNodeAffinity, _ = nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod).Match(node)\\n1308: \\t_, hasUntoleratedTaint := v1helper.FindMatchingUntoleratedTaint(taints, pod.Spec.Tolerations, func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n1309: \\t\\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule\\n1310: \\t})\\n1311: \\tfitsTaints = !hasUntoleratedTaint\\n1312: \\treturn\\n1313: }\\n1314: \\n1315: // NewPod creates a new pod\\n1316: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/controller/daemon/daemon_controller.go%3FL1290-1315\"}"

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: const (\\n3510: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3511: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3512: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3513: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3514: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3515: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3516: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3517: \\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3518: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3519: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3520: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3521: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3506-3528\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3506: // +enum\n3507: type TaintEffect string\n3508: \n3509: const (\n3510: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3511: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3512: \t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n3513: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3514: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3515: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3516: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3517: \t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\n3518: \tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n3519: \t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n3520: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n3521: \t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3506-3528"
  }
}

61. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/generate_modifications.sh << 'EOF'\n#!/bin/bash\n\n# This script generates all the modifications needed for NoScheduleNoTraffic support\n\necho \"Creating modification patches for NoScheduleNoTraffic support...\"\n\n# 1. Add TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic to constants sections\n\n# For staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go and pkg/apis/core/types.go\n# Replace the existing TaintEffectNoExecute constant with both the existing one and the new one\n\ncat > /tmp/taint_effect_constants.txt << 'CONSTANTS'\n\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\n\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\n\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\n\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\nCONSTANTS\n\necho \"Generated taint effect constants\"\n\n# 2. Generate validation function update for pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\n\ncat > /tmp/validation_switch.txt << 'VALIDATION'\n\tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic:\nVALIDATION\n\ncat > /tmp/validation_values.txt << 'VALUES'\n\t\tvalidValues := []core.TaintEffect{\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t}\nVALUES\n\necho \"Generated validation switch statements\"\n\n# 3. Generate scheduler filter update for pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go\n\ncat > /tmp/scheduler_filter.txt << 'FILTER'\n\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is interested in NoSchedule, NoExecute, and NoScheduleNoTraffic taints.\n\t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic\n\t}\nFILTER\n\necho \"Generated scheduler filter update\"\n\n# 4. Generate taint util validation update for pkg/util/taints/taints.go\n\ncat > /tmp/taints_util_validation.txt << 'UTIL_VALIDATION'\n\tif effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\nUTIL_VALIDATION\n\necho \"Generated taint util validation\"\n\n# 5. Generate kubectl taint validation update for staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go\n\ncat > /tmp/kubectl_taint_validation.txt << 'KUBECTL_VALIDATION'\n\tif effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\nKUBECTL_VALIDATION\n\necho \"Generated kubectl taint validation\"\n\n# 6. Generate endpoint slice node taint checker\n\ncat > /tmp/endpoint_node_taint_check.txt << 'ENDPOINT_CHECK'\n// nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint returns true if the node has a NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\nfunc nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node *v1.Node) bool {\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\nENDPOINT_CHECK\n\necho \"Generated endpoint node taint check\"\n\necho \"All modification templates generated successfully\"\nEOF\n\nchmod +x /tmp/generate_modifications.sh\n/tmp/generate_modifications.sh\n"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Creating modification patches for NoScheduleNoTraffic support...\nGenerated taint effect constants\nGenerated validation switch statements\nGenerated scheduler filter update\nGenerated taint util validation\nGenerated kubectl taint validation\nGenerated endpoint node taint check\nAll modification templates generated successfully"
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3520: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3521: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \\n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\\n3532: type Toleration struct {\\n3533: \\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\\n3534: \\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\\n3535: \\t// +optional\\n3536: \\tKey string `json:\\\"key,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n3537: \\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\\n3538: \\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\\n3539: \\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\\n3540: \\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\\n3541: \\t// +optional\\n3542: \\tOperator TolerationOperator `json:\\\"operator,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=TolerationOperator\\\"`\\n3543: \\t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\\n3544: \\t// If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\\n3545: \\t// +optional\\n3546: \\tValue string `json:\\\"value,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\\\"`\\n3547: \\t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\\n3548: \\t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\\n3549: \\t// +optional\\n3550: \\tEffect TaintEffect `json:\\\"effect,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=TaintEffect\\\"`\\n3551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3520-3550\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3520: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n3521: \t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: \n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n3532: type Toleration struct {\n3533: \t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n3534: \t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n3535: \t// +optional\n3536: \tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n3537: \t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n3538: \t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n3539: \t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\n3540: \t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n3541: \t// +optional\n3542: \tOperator TolerationOperator `json:\"operator,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=TolerationOperator\"`\n3543: \t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\n3544: \t// If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\n3545: \t// +optional\n3546: \tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\"`\n3547: \t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\n3548: \t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\n3549: \t// +optional\n3550: \tEffect TaintEffect `json:\"effect,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=TaintEffect\"`\n3551: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3520-3550"
  }

64. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fetch_and_patch.py << 'PYTHON'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\nimport subprocess\nimport re\nimport os\n\n# Dictionary of files to fetch and patch information\nfiles_to_patch = {\n    \"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\": [\n        {\n            \"search\": 'TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\\n)',\n            \"replace\": 'TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\\n\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\\n\\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\\n\\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n\\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\\n\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\\n)'\n        },\n        {\n            \"search\": \"// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\",\n            \"replace\": \"// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, NoExecute, and NoScheduleNoTraffic.\"\n        }\n    ],\n    \"pkg/apis/core/types.go\": [\n        {\n            \"search\": 'TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\\n)',\n            \"replace\": 'TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\\n\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from the Service\\n\\t// EndpointSlices to prevent traffic from being routed to the node.\\n\\t// Does not evict already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n\\t// Enforced by the scheduler and the EndpointSlice controller.\\n\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\\n)'\n        }\n    ]\n}\n\nprint(\"Python script ready. Files to patch:\")\nfor file_path in files_to_patch.keys():\n    print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n\nPYTHON\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_patch.py\n"
}

65. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "package main\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n)\n\n// TestNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint tests the NoScheduleNoTraffic taint effect\nfunc TestNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\ttaint    v1.Taint\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\",\n\t\t\ttaint: v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tKey:    \"dedicated\",\n\t\t\t\tValue:  \"gpu\",\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tif string(test.taint.Effect) != test.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %q, got %q\", test.expected, test.taint.Effect)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestNoScheduleNoTrafficScheduling tests that pods don't get scheduled on nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\nfunc TestNoScheduleNoTrafficScheduling(t *testing.T) {\n\tnode := &v1.Node{}\n\tnode.Spec.Taints = []v1.Taint{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tKey:    \"dedicated\",\n\t\t\tValue:  \"gpu\",\n\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tpod := &v1.Pod{}\n\tpod.Spec.Tolerations = []v1.Toleration{} // No tolerations\n\n\t// Pod should be rejected on node with NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\n\tt.Run(\"pod without toleration should not be scheduled\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// This should match the taint\n\t\tmatches := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\t\tmatches = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matches {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected NoScheduleNoTraffic taint to be present on node\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestNoScheduleNoTrafficEndpoints tests that nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic are excluded from endpoints\nfunc TestNoScheduleNoTrafficEndpoints(t *testing.T) {\n\tnode := &v1.Node{}\n\tnode.Name = \"test-node\"\n\tnode.Spec.Taints = []v1.Taint{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tKey:    \"drain\",\n\t\t\tValue:  \"true\",\n\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tpod := &v1.Pod{}\n\tpod.Spec.NodeName = \"test-node\"\n\n\t// Pod on node with NoScheduleNoTraffic should not be included in endpoints\n\t// (unless it explicitly tolerates it)\n\tt.Run(\"pods on NoScheduleNoTraffic node should be excluded from endpoints\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\thasNoScheduleNoTraffic := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\t\thasNoScheduleNoTraffic = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !hasNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected node to have NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_noschedulenotraffic.go"
}
Output
{
  "content": "package main\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n)\n\n// TestNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint tests the NoScheduleNoTraffic taint effect\nfunc TestNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\ttaint    v1.Taint\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\",\n\t\t\ttaint: v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tKey:    \"dedicated\",\n\t\t\t\tValue:  \"gpu\",\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: TestNoScheduleNoTrafficScheduling(t *testing.T) {\n\tnode := &v1.Node{}\n\tnode.Spec.Taints = []v1.Taint{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tKey:    \"dedicated\",\n\t\t\tValue:  \"gpu\",\n\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tpod := &v1.Pod{}\n\tpod.Spec.Tolerations = []v1.Toleration{} // No tolerations\n\n\t// Pod should be rejected on node with NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\n\tt.Run(\"pod without toleration should not be scheduled\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// This should match the taint\n\t\tmatches := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\t\tmatches = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matches {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected NoScheduleNoTraffic taint to be present on node\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestNoScheduleNoTrafficEndpoints tests that nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic are excluded from endpoints\nfunc TestNoScheduleNoTrafficEndpoints(t *testing.T) {\n\tnode := &v1.Node{}\n\tnode.Name = \"test-node\"\n\tnode.Spec.Taints = []v1.Taint{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tKey:    \"drain\",\n\t\t\tValue:  \"true\",\n\t\t\tEffect: (unless it explicitly tolerates it)\n\tt.Run(\"pods on NoScheduleNoTraffic node should be excluded from endpoints\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\thasNoScheduleNoTraffic := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\t\thasNoScheduleNoTraffic = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !hasNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected node to have NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n",

    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package helper\n18: \n19: import v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n20: \n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n24: \treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n25: \t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\n26: \t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\n27: \t}\n28: }\n29: ",

67. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\n// DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n// filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\nfunc DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is interested in NoSchedule, NoExecute, and NoScheduleNoTraffic taints.\n\t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic\n\t}\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg_scheduler_framework_plugins_helper_taint.go"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\n// DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n// filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\nfunc DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is interested in NoSchedule, NoExecute, and NoScheduleNoTraffic taints.\n\t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic\n\t}\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/pkg_scheduler_framework_plugins_helper_taint.go",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

Input
{
  "endLine": 73,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"34: )\\n35: \\n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\\n38: \\tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\\n39: \\tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\\n40: \\t// For compatibility reasons, \\\"ready\\\" should never be \\\"true\\\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\\n41: \\t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\\n42: \\tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\\n43: \\tep := discovery.Endpoint{\\n44: \\t\\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\\n45: \\t\\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\\n46: \\t\\t\\tReady:       &ready,\\n47: \\t\\t\\tServing:     &serving,\\n48: \\t\\t\\tTerminating: &terminating,\\n49: \\t\\t},\\n50: \\t\\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\\n51: \\t\\t\\tKind:      \\\"Pod\\\",\\n52: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\\n53: \\t\\t\\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\\n54: \\t\\t\\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\\n55: \\t\\t},\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \\\"\\\" {\\n59: \\t\\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\\n60: \\t}\\n61: \\n62: \\tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \\\"\\\" {\\n63: \\t\\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\\n64: \\t\\tep.Zone = &zone\\n65: \\t}\\n66: \\n67: \\tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\\n68: \\t\\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\\n69: \\t}\\n70: \\n71: \\treturn ep\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go%3FL34-73\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "34: )\n35: \n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n39: \tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n40: \t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n41: \t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\n42: \tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\n43: \tep := discovery.Endpoint{\n44: \t\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\n45: \t\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\n46: \t\t\tReady:       &ready,\n47: \t\t\tServing:     &serving,\n48: \t\t\tTerminating: &terminating,\n49: \t\t},\n50: \t\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\n51: \t\t\tKind:      \"Pod\",\n52: \t\t\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\n53: \t\t\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\n54: \t\t\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\n55: \t\t},\n56: \t}\n57: \n58: \tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" {\n59: \t\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\n60: \t}\n61: \n62: \tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \"\" {\n63: \t\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\n64: \t\tep.Zone = &zone\n65: \t}\n66: \n67: \tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\n68: \t\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\n69: \t}\n70: \n71: \treturn ep\n72: }\n73: \n74: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go%3FL34-73"
  }
}

{
  "endLine": 215,
